- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- commit: Stage and commit changes with a conventional commit message
- improve-codebase-architecture: Find deepening opportunities in a codebase, informed by the domain language in CONTEXT.md and the decisions in docs/adr/. Use when the user wants to improve architecture, find refactoring opportunities, consolidate tightly-coupled modules, or make a codebase more testable and AI-navigable.
- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- triage: Triage issues through a state machine driven by triage roles. Use when user wants to create an issue, triage issues, review incoming bugs or feature requests, prepare issues for an AFK agent, or manage issue workflow.
- prototype: Build a throwaway prototype to flush out a design before committing to it. Routes between two branches — a runnable terminal app for state/business-logic questions, or several radically different UI variations toggleable from one route. Use when the user wants to prototype, sanity-check a data model or state machine, mock up a UI, explore design options, or says "prototype this", "let me play with it", "try a few designs".
- diagnose: Disciplined diagnosis loop for hard bugs and performance regressions. Reproduce → minimise → hypothesise → instrument → fix → regression-test. Use when user says "diagnose this" / "debug this", reports a bug, says something is broken/throwing/failing, or describes a performance regression.
- to-issues: Break a plan, spec, or PRD into independently-grabbable issues on the project issue tracker using tracer-bullet vertical slices. Use when user wants to convert a plan into issues, create implementation tickets, or break down work into issues.
- write-a-skill: Create new agent skills with proper structure, progressive disclosure, and bundled resources. Use when user wants to create, write, or build a new skill.
- grill-with-docs: Grilling session that challenges your plan against the existing domain model, sharpens terminology, and updates documentation (CONTEXT.md, ADRs) inline as decisions crystallise. Use when user wants to stress-test a plan against their project's language and documented decisions.
- caveman: Ultra-compressed communication mode. Cuts token usage ~75% by dropping filler, articles, and pleasantries while keeping full technical accuracy. Use when user says "caveman mode", "talk like caveman", "use caveman", "less tokens", "be brief", or invokes /caveman.
- tdd: Test-driven development with red-green-refactor loop. Use when user wants to build features or fix bugs using TDD, mentions "red-green-refactor", wants integration tests, or asks for test-first development.
- grill-me: Interview the user relentlessly about a plan or design until reaching shared understanding, resolving each branch of the decision tree. Use when user wants to stress-test a plan, get grilled on their design, or mentions "grill me".
- to-prd: Turn the current conversation context into a PRD and publish it to the project issue tracker. Use when user wants to create a PRD from the current context.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the publish feature. Let me compile the report:

## Publish Feature Architecture Report: AgentCenter

### 1. File Structure & Layout

**Page routes:**
- `/app/[locale]/publish/page.tsx` — Dashboard listing user's extensions with status and rejection reasons
- `/app/[locale]/publish/new/page.tsx` — Entry point to create a new extension draft (step 1 of wizard)
- `/app/[locale]/publish/[id]/edit/page.tsx` — Resume an in-flight draft
- `/app/[locale]/publish/error.tsx`, `loading.tsx` — Error and loading boundaries

**UI Components:**
- `/components/publish/upload-wizard.tsx` — Three-step multi-step wizard (single React component with local step state)
- `/components/publish/manifest-form.tsx` — Form for step 1 (extension details)
- `/components/publish/discard-button.tsx` — Delete draft confirmation button
- `/components/publish/dashboard-skeleton.tsx`, `wizard-skeleton.tsx` — Suspense loaders

**Server Actions (all in `/lib/actions/publish.ts`, "use server"):**
- `createDraftExtension(ManifestFormValues)` — Create extension + version in DB
- `attachFile(versionId, r2Key, size, checksumSha256)` — Record uploaded bundle in `files` table
- `submitForReview(versionId)` — Transition version to `scanning` and queue Inngest job
- `updateDraftExtension(extensionId, ManifestFormValues)` — Edit manifest while `status === "pending"`
- `discardDraft(extensionId)` — Delete draft extension (owner-checked, draft-only)
- `getDraft(extensionId)` — Load draft for resume mode (returns full form values)
- `getMyExtensions(userId)` — Dashboard query with latest version status and scan rejection reason
- `getVersionStatus(versionId)` — Simple status lookup

**API Routes:**
- `/app/api/upload/sign/route.ts` — POST handler to generate R2 presigned PUT URL

**Library code:**
- `/lib/storage/r2.ts` — R2 client functions: `generatePresignedPutUrl()`, `generatePresignedGetUrl()`, `bundleKey(slug, version)`
- `/lib/jobs/scan-bundle.ts` — Inngest function that downloads, checksums, extracts manifest, validates against `BundleManifestSchema`
- `/lib/publish/scan-report.ts` — `extractScanReason()` defensive JSON extractor
- `/lib/publish/row-action.ts` — Pure state-routing logic: maps (status, bundleUploaded) to action enum
- `/lib/validators/manifest.ts` — Zod schemas: `ExtensionManifestCore`, `ManifestFormSchema`, `BundleManifestSchema`
- `/lib/actions/publish-errors.ts` — Error classification from Drizzle/node-postgres exceptions
- `/lib/extensions/state.ts` — State machine: `submit()`, `recordScanResult()`, `publishVersion()` with transition validation

---

### 2. Publish UI & Step Flow

**Single Multi-Step Wizard (not separate pages):**
The `UploadWizard` component is a **single-page three-step flow** managed entirely in React client state. No server navigation between steps.

**Step flow:**
1. **Step 1: Extension Details** (`ManifestForm`)
   - Text inputs: slug, name (EN/ZH), version (semver), tagline
   - Selects: category, scope, funcCat
   - Text input: subCat, l2 (optional)
   - Multiselect: tags (up to 8)
   - Selects: deptId (optional department)
   - Textareas: description (EN/ZH)
   - URLs: homepageUrl, repoUrl
   - Text: licenseSpdx (SPDX code)
   - **Validation:** Zod `ManifestFormSchema` on client (before server action) + server-side re-validation in action
   - **On submit:** Calls `createDraftExtension()` (new) or `updateDraftExtension()` (resume mode with locked slug/version). Advances to Step 2.

2. **Step 2: File Upload**
   - Drag-drop zone (50 MB max ZIP)
   - Client-side validation: filename, size
   - **Upload flow:**
     - POST `/api/upload/sign` with slug, version, contentType, size → returns `{ uploadUrl, r2Key }`
     - PUT directly to presigned R2 URL with file body
     - Server action `attachFile()` records in `files` table with `scanStatus: "pending"` and checksum placeholder
   - **On success:** "Uploaded" checksum icon + "Next" button enabled → Step 3
   - **In resume mode:** If bundle already uploaded, shows "Uploaded" and skips to Step 3

3. **Step 3: Review & Submit**
   - Read-only summary: slug, version, bundle checksum status (green ✓)
   - "Back" and "Submit for review" buttons
   - **On submit:** Calls `submitForReview(versionId)` → transitions status to `scanning`, queues Inngest `extension/scan.requested` job
   - **Success page:** Checkmark + "Submitted!" message + link back to dashboard

**Resume Mode:**
- Triggered via `/publish/[id]/edit` page with `getDraft()` result
- Skips Step 1 if bundle already uploaded; lands on Step 2 (or Step 3 if both slug/version and bundle exist)
- Manifest form renders with locked `slug` + `version` fields (styled `readOnly` in input, security enforced in server action)
- Resume banner at top shows extension name and "Back to dashboard" link

**Error handling:**
- Client-side catches network/size/type errors during upload
- Server action error responses include `{ ok: false, error: "code", detail?: "message" }`
- Error codes map to i18n keys in `publish.errors.*`
- Development mode includes error detail (pg code, constraint, SQL); production hides it

---

### 3. Supporting Components in `/components/publish/**`

| Component | Responsibility |
|-----------|-----------------|
| **upload-wizard.tsx** | Orchestrates all three steps. Manages: step state, draft state (extension/version IDs), file upload state, error state. Wires up form submit, file change, and submit button handlers. Routes between steps. Shows success page. ~420 lines. |
| **manifest-form.tsx** | Step 1 form UI. Renders all manifest fields with Zod validation on client. Accepts `defaultValues`, `lockedFields` array, optional `submitLabel` override. Self-contained validation + submit. ~280 lines. |
| **discard-button.tsx** | Trashed-only UI for draft rows on dashboard. Confirms via `window.confirm()`, calls `discardDraft()`, refreshes page via `router.refresh()`. Shows error inline. |
| **dashboard-skeleton.tsx**, **wizard-skeleton.tsx** | Suspense loader skeletons for streaming RSC boundaries. |

---

### 4. Server-Side Implementation

**Database schema (`/lib/db/schema/extension.ts`):**

| Table | Key Columns | Publish-Relevant |
|-------|-------------|-----------------|
| `extensions` | id (PK), slug (unique), name, nameZh, visibility (enum: draft/published/archived), publisherUserId (FK→users), ownerOrgId (FK→orgs), deptId (FK→depts), category, scope, funcCat, subCat, l2, tagline, description, descriptionZh, homepageUrl, repoUrl, licenseSpdx | All core fields written by `createDraftExtension()` and `updateDraftExtension()` |
| `extensionVersions` | id (PK), extensionId (FK), version, status (enum: pending/scanning/ready/rejected), bundleFileId (logical ref, no FK), publishedAt | Status drives dashboard state machine. `bundleFileId` links to `files.id`. Unique(extensionId, version) prevents duplicate versions. |
| `files` | id (PK), r2Key, size, checksumSha256, mimeType, scanStatus (enum: pending/clean/flagged), scanReport (jsonb) | `scanReport` holds `{ manifestOk, reason, checksum, scannedAt }` from Inngest scan job. Dashboard joins via `extensionVersions.bundleFileId`. |
| `extensionTags` | extensionId (FK), tagId (FK), PK(both) | Many-to-many. Wizard form caps at 8 tags; `updateDraftExtension` deletes + re-inserts wholesale. |

**Row-state machine (`/lib/publish/row-action.ts`)**

Maps (versionStatus, bundleUploaded) → action enum for dashboard rendering:
- `pending` + no bundle → `resume_upload` (clickable, lands on Step 2)
- `pending` + bundle → `resume_submit` (clickable, lands on Step 3)
- `scanning` → `scanning` (non-clickable)
- `ready` → `ready` (non-clickable, awaiting manual publish)
- `rejected` → `rejected` (non-clickable, but rejection reason now shown)
- null/unknown → `none`

**Scan job (`/lib/jobs/scan-bundle.ts`)**

Inngest function (`scan-bundle`, triggered by `extension/scan.requested` event):
1. Fetches bundle from R2 via presigned GET URL
2. Computes SHA-256 checksum
3. Unzips and extracts `manifest.toml`
4. Parses TOML and validates against `BundleManifestSchema` (not the form schema — bundle omits deptId, tags, URLs)
5. Records result via `recordScanResult()`:
   - If valid: sets `files.scanStatus = "clean"`, `extensionVersions.status = "ready"`
   - If invalid: sets `files.scanStatus = "flagged"`, `extensionVersions.status = "rejected"`, reason in `files.scanReport.reason`
6. On success, emits `extension/index.requested` for search indexing

**Error classification (`/lib/actions/publish-errors.ts`)**

Wraps Drizzle/node-postgres exceptions and maps to stable codes:
- `23505` unique_violation → `slug_taken` or `version_taken`
- `23503` foreign_key_violation → `invalid_dept`, `invalid_tag`, `org_missing`, or `invalid_reference`
- `23502` not_null_violation → `missing_required`

**State transitions (`/lib/extensions/state.ts:submit()`, `recordScanResult()`)**

- `submit()`: pending or scanning → scanning (idempotent, for retries)
- `recordScanResult()`: scanning → ready or rejected
- Throws `VersionStateError` if row missing or not in expected state (Inngest catches, no-ops)

**Dashboard rejection display**

Recent commit `213d953`:
- `getMyExtensions()` now joins `files` via logical `extensionVersions.bundleFileId` to fetch `scanReport`
- `extractScanReason()` defensively extracts `report.reason` string (returns null if missing/malformed)
- Dashboard page: when row action is `rejected` and reason present, renders inline below the row in destructive red
- i18n key: `publish.dashboard.rejectedReasonLabel`

---

### 5. Server Actions & Route Handlers (with exports)

**Server Actions (`/lib/actions/publish.ts`, all "use server"):**

| Export | Signature | Purpose |
|--------|-----------|---------|
| `createDraftExtension` | `(raw: ManifestFormValues) → Promise<CreateDraftResult>` | Step 1 submit: create extension + version, validate schema, generate UUIDs. Owner: session.user.id |
| `updateDraftExtension` | `(extensionId: string, raw: ManifestFormValues) → Promise<UpdateDraftResult>` | Edit existing draft. Refuses non-pending versions. Slug/version NOT written (security). Owner-checked. |
| `attachFile` | `(versionId: string, r2Key: string, size: number, checksumSha256: string) → Promise<AttachFileResult>` | Step 2: record uploaded bundle. Creates `files` row, updates `extensionVersions.bundleFileId`. |
| `submitForReview` | `(versionId: string) → Promise<SubmitResult>` | Step 3: transition to scanning, send Inngest event, rollback to pending if event send fails. |
| `discardDraft` | `(extensionId: string) → Promise<DiscardDraftResult>` | Delete draft (visibility == "draft" only). Owner-checked. R2 objects left for bucket lifecycle. |
| `getDraft` | `(extensionId: string) → Promise<GetDraftResult>` | Load draft for resume/edit page. Returns full form values + bundle upload state. Owner-checked. |
| `getMyExtensions` | `(userId: string) → Promise<[Extension & { latestScanReason }]>` | Dashboard: all extensions, latest version + scan reason per extension. |
| `getVersionStatus` | `(versionId: string) → Promise<string \| null>` | Simple status lookup. |

**Route Handlers:**

| Route | Method | Purpose |
|-------|--------|---------|
| `/api/upload/sign` | POST | Generate R2 presigned PUT URL. Accepts slug, version, contentType, size. Returns `{ uploadUrl, r2Key }`. Auth via Bearer token (future CLI) or session cookie. |

**Inngest Functions (`/lib/jobs/index.ts` exports):**

| Export | Trigger | Purpose |
|--------|---------|---------|
| `scanBundle` | `extension/scan.requested` | Download from R2, checksum, validate manifest, record result. |
| `reindexSearch` | `extension/index.requested` | (Not detailed in this mapping; search-related) |

---

### 6. i18n Keys (`messages/en.json` & `messages/zh.json`)

**Publish namespace: `publish.*`**

| Key Path | Usage |
|----------|-------|
| `publish.dashboard.title` | "My Extensions" |
| `publish.dashboard.statusDraft`, `.statusPublished`, `.statusArchived` | Extension visibility badges |
| `publish.dashboard.versionPendingUpload`, `.versionReadyToSubmit`, `.versionScanning`, `.versionReady`, `.versionRejected` | Status labels |
| `publish.dashboard.rejectedReasonLabel` | "Reason" (NEW in commit 213d953) |
| `publish.dashboard.empty`, `.emptyTitle`, `.emptyCta`, `.newButton` | Empty state + CTA |
| `publish.wizard.step1Title`, `.step2Title`, `.step3Title` | Step headers |
| `publish.wizard.nextButton`, `.backButton`, `.submitButton`, `.submitting` | Button labels + loading |
| `publish.wizard.successTitle`, `.successBody`, `.backToDashboard`, `.resumingLabel`, `.saveAndContinue` | Success page, resume banner |
| `publish.form.slug`, `.name`, `.nameZh`, `.version`, `.category`, `.scope`, `.funcCat`, `.subCat`, `.l2`, `.dept`, `.tags`, `.description`, `.descriptionZh`, `.tagline`, `.homepageUrl`, `.repoUrl`, `.licenseSpdx`, `.bundle` | Form field labels |
| `publish.form.slugHint`, `.versionHint`, `.tagsHint`, `.lockedHint` | Helper text |
| `publish.upload.dropzone`, `.maxSize`, `.uploading`, `.uploaded` | Upload zone |
| `publish.upload.errorSize`, `.errorType` | File validation errors |
| `publish.errors.unauthenticated`, `.invalid_input`, `.slug_taken`, `.version_taken`, `.unique_conflict`, `.invalid_dept`, `.invalid_tag`, `.org_missing`, `.invalid_reference`, `.missing_required`, `.no_bundle`, `.version_not_editable`, `.version_not_submittable`, `.scan_queue_unavailable`, `.db_error`, `.fallback` | Server action errors |
| `publish.errors.uploadSign`, `.uploadFailed` | R2 upload errors |
| `publish.discard.ariaLabel`, `.confirm`, `.error_not_found`, `.error_not_discardable`, `.error_unauthenticated`, `.error_fallback` | Discard button + confirm dialog |

---

### 7. Tests

| File | Coverage |
|------|----------|
| `/components/publish/manifest-form.test.tsx` | Form validation, field rendering (basic snapshot/behavior) |
| `/lib/publish/row-action.test.ts` | State routing logic: (status, bundleUploaded) → action enum |
| `/lib/publish/scan-report.test.ts` | `extractScanReason()` defensive JSON parsing |
| `/lib/actions/publish-ownership.test.ts` | Security regressions: `getDraft` + `discardDraft` ownership collapse, `updateDraftExtension` slug/version immutability |
| `/lib/actions/publish-errors.test.ts` | (File exists but not read; likely error code mapping) |

None are end-to-end integration tests; DB-touching paths rely on manual testing or integration suite.

---

### 8. Feature Flags, Environment Variables & CLAUDE.md Notes

**Environment variables (from `.env.example`):**
- `DATABASE_URL` — Postgres connection (Neon)
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` — Public app URL for i18n link generation
- `R2_ACCOUNT_ID`, `R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, `R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`, `R2_BUCKET` — Cloudflare R2 credentials
- `INNGEST_EVENT_KEY` — Inngest cloud event key (dev: skipped if `NODE_ENV !== "production"`, routes to local dev server at `localhost:8288`)
- `INNGEST_SIGNING_KEY` — Webhook signing (not typically needed in production)

**CLAUDE.md locked decisions affecting publish:**
- Multi-tenant schema, single-tenant UI (v1)
- Department IDs: dotted-path text PKs (e.g., `eng.cloud.infra`)
- Dynamic content i18n: column-per-language (EN + ZH)
- R2 for bundle storage
- Inngest for background jobs
- Better Auth for sessions (cookie-based)

**No explicit feature flags** in the publish code; scan results and submissions are always enabled. Dev/prod distinction: Inngest routes to local dev server if `NODE_ENV !== "production"`.

---

### 9. Dashboard Rejection Reason Surface (Commit 213d953)

**Change summary:** Rejected extensions are no longer dead-ends; the rejection reason is now visible inline on the dashboard.

**Implementation:**
- **Query change:** `getMyExtensions()` joins `files` table via logical `extensionVersions.bundleFileId` reference to fetch `files.scanReport` JSONB column
- **Extractor:** New `extractScanReason()` function in `/lib/publish/scan-report.ts` defensively reads `report.reason` (handles null, malformed JSON, missing keys)
- **Dashboard render:** When row action is `rejected` AND reason is present, renders a second row below the extension entry:
  ```
  border-t border-border px-5 py-3 text-xs text-destructive
  "Reason: {extractedReason}"
  ```
- **i18n:** New key `publish.dashboard.rejectedReasonLabel` ("Reason" in EN, "原因" in ZH)

This prevents users from abandoning rejected drafts without knowing why. The reason comes from `scan-bundle.ts` scan failures (missing manifest, invalid TOML, schema validation errors, etc.) stored in the `scanReport` JSONB column.

---

### Summary Table: Full Surface Area

| Layer | Files | Key Exports/Routes |
|-------|-------|-------------------|
| **Pages** | 3 page files + error/loading | `/publish`, `/publish/new`, `/publish/[id]/edit` |
| **Client Components** | 6 components (upload-wizard, manifest-form, discard-button, skeletons) | UploadWizard (3-step state), ManifestForm, DiscardButton |
| **Server Actions** | publish.ts (1 file) | 8 exported actions: create, update, attach, submit, discard, get, getMyExtensions, getVersionStatus |
| **API Routes** | upload/sign/route.ts | POST /api/upload/sign |
| **Jobs** | scan-bundle.ts + client.ts | scanBundle (Inngest fn) |
| **Storage** | r2.ts | generatePresignedPutUrl(), bundleKey(), generatePresignedGetUrl() |
| **Validators** | manifest.ts | ExtensionManifestCore, ManifestFormSchema, BundleManifestSchema |
| **State Machine** | extensions/state.ts | submit(), recordScanResult(), publishVersion() |
| **Helpers** | row-action.ts, scan-report.ts, publish-errors.ts | rowAction(), extractScanReason(), classifyDraftError() |
| **Tests** | 5 test files | ownership security, state routing, JSON parsing, error classification |
| **i18n** | messages/{en,zh}.json | 40+ keys under `publish.*` namespace |
| **DB Schema** | extension.ts | extensions, extensionVersions, files, extensionTags tables |

This design separates concerns cleanly: UI state lives in React, persistent state in Postgres, async work in Inngest, presigned URLs via R2 API. The wizard is resumable at any step (with form pre-fill from DB), and scan rejection reasons are surfaced inline to unblock user workflows.

toolUseResult.prompt:
I'm planning a redesign of the publish feature in the AgentCenter Next.js project at /Users/hutusi/workspace/ai/naive/agentcenter. Map the existing publish feature thoroughly.

Specifically:

1. List every file under `app/[locale]/publish/**`, `components/publish/**`, `lib/**` related to publish/uploads/scan, and `app/api/**` endpoints used by publish (presigned upload, scan, submit-for-review, status polling, etc.).

2. Read the publish UI page(s) and describe: current step flow (single page vs multi-step), what form fields exist, how the bundle upload works (R2 presigned upload), how scan results are surfaced, how the submit-for-review action wires up, and how status polling/refresh is currently done.

3. Read the supporting components in `components/publish/**` and describe each one's responsibility.

4. Read the relevant server-side code: `lib/jobs/**` for Inngest scan/index jobs, `lib/storage/**` for R2 presign, `lib/db/schema.ts` for `extension`, `extensionVersion`, `scanReport` (or similar), and `lib/validators/**` for manifest validation.

5. List Server Actions and route handlers used by publish (file paths + exported names).

6. Note current i18n keys for publish (look in `messages/en.json`, `messages/zh.json`).

7. Note current tests for publish (under `tests/**` or `__tests__/**`).

8. Identify any feature flags, env vars, and dashboards mentioned in CLAUDE.md or memory that affect publish (e.g. Inngest dev server, R2 CORS).

9. Include a paragraph on dashboard surfaces that show scan rejection reason (recent commit `213d953` mentions this).

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the publish feature. Let me compile the report:

## Publish Feature Architecture Report: AgentCenter

### 1. File Structure & Layout

**Page routes:**
- `/app/[locale]/publish/page.tsx` — Dashboard listing user's extensions with status and rejection reasons
- `/app/[locale]/publish/new/page.tsx` — Entry point to create a new extension draft (step 1 of wizard)
- `/app/[locale]/publish/[id]/edit/page.tsx` — Resume an in-flight draft
- `/app/[locale]/publish/error.tsx`, `loading.tsx` — Error and loading boundaries

**UI Components:**
- `/components/publish/upload-wizard.tsx` — Three-step multi-step wizard (single React component with local step state)
- `/components/publish/manifest-form.tsx` — Form for step 1 (extension details)
- `/components/publish/discard-button.tsx` — Delete draft confirmation button
- `/components/publish/dashboard-skeleton.tsx`, `wizard-skeleton.tsx` — Suspense loaders

**Server Actions (all in `/lib/actions/publish.ts`, "use server"):**
- `createDraftExtension(ManifestFormValues)` — Create extension + version in DB
- `attachFile(versionId, r2Key, size, checksumSha256)` — Record uploaded bundle in `files` table
- `submitForReview(versionId)` — Transition version to `scanning` and queue Inngest job
- `updateDraftExtension(extensionId, ManifestFormValues)` — Edit manifest while `status === "pending"`
- `discardDraft(extensionId)` — Delete draft extension (owner-checked, draft-only)
- `getDraft(extensionId)` — Load draft for resume mode (returns full form values)
- `getMyExtensions(userId)` — Dashboard query with latest version status and scan rejection reason
- `getVersionStatus(versionId)` — Simple status lookup

**API Routes:**
- `/app/api/upload/sign/route.ts` — POST handler to generate R2 presigned PUT URL

**Library code:**
- `/lib/storage/r2.ts` — R2 client functions: `generatePresignedPutUrl()`, `generatePresignedGetUrl()`, `bundleKey(slug, version)`
- `/lib/jobs/scan-bundle.ts` — Inngest function that downloads, checksums, extracts manifest, validates against `BundleManifestSchema`
- `/lib/publish/scan-report.ts` — `extractScanReason()` defensive JSON extractor
- `/lib/publish/row-action.ts` — Pure state-routing logic: maps (status, bundleUploaded) to action enum
- `/lib/validators/manifest.ts` — Zod schemas: `ExtensionManifestCore`, `ManifestFormSchema`, `BundleManifestSchema`
- `/lib/actions/publish-errors.ts` — Error classification from Drizzle/node-postgres exceptions
- `/lib/extensions/state.ts` — State machine: `submit()`, `recordScanResult()`, `publishVersion()` with transition validation

---

### 2. Publish UI & Step Flow

**Single Multi-Step Wizard (not separate pages):**
The `UploadWizard` component is a **single-page three-step flow** managed entirely in React client state. No server navigation between steps.

**Step flow:**
1. **Step 1: Extension Details** (`ManifestForm`)
   - Text inputs: slug, name (EN/ZH), version (semver), tagline
   - Selects: category, scope, funcCat
   - Text input: subCat, l2 (optional)
   - Multiselect: tags (up to 8)
   - Selects: deptId (optional department)
   - Textareas: description (EN/ZH)
   - URLs: homepageUrl, repoUrl
   - Text: licenseSpdx (SPDX code)
   - **Validation:** Zod `ManifestFormSchema` on client (before server action) + server-side re-validation in action
   - **On submit:** Calls `createDraftExtension()` (new) or `updateDraftExtension()` (resume mode with locked slug/version). Advances to Step 2.

2. **Step 2: File Upload**
   - Drag-drop zone (50 MB max ZIP)
   - Client-side validation: filename, size
   - **Upload flow:**
     - POST `/api/upload/sign` with slug, version, contentType, size → returns `{ uploadUrl, r2Key }`
     - PUT directly to presigned R2 URL with file body
     - Server action `attachFile()` records in `files` table with `scanStatus: "pending"` and checksum placeholder
   - **On success:** "Uploaded" checksum icon + "Next" button enabled → Step 3
   - **In resume mode:** If bundle already uploaded, shows "Uploaded" and skips to Step 3

3. **Step 3: Review & Submit**
   - Read-only summary: slug, version, bundle checksum status (green ✓)
   - "Back" and "Submit for review" buttons
   - **On submit:** Calls `submitForReview(versionId)` → transitions status to `scanning`, queues Inngest `extension/scan.requested` job
   - **Success page:** Checkmark + "Submitted!" message + link back to dashboard

**Resume Mode:**
- Triggered via `/publish/[id]/edit` page with `getDraft()` result
- Skips Step 1 if bundle already uploaded; lands on Step 2 (or Step 3 if both slug/version and bundle exist)
- Manifest form renders with locked `slug` + `version` fields (styled `readOnly` in input, security enforced in server action)
- Resume banner at top shows extension name and "Back to dashboard" link

**Error handling:**
- Client-side catches network/size/type errors during upload
- Server action error responses include `{ ok: false, error: "code", detail?: "message" }`
- Error codes map to i18n keys in `publish.errors.*`
- Development mode includes error detail (pg code, constraint, SQL); production hides it

---

### 3. Supporting Components in `/components/publish/**`

| Component | Responsibility |
|-----------|-----------------|
| **upload-wizard.tsx** | Orchestrates all three steps. Manages: step state, draft state (extension/version IDs), file upload state, error state. Wires up form submit, file change, and submit button handlers. Routes between steps. Shows success page. ~420 lines. |
| **manifest-form.tsx** | Step 1 form UI. Renders all manifest fields with Zod validation on client. Accepts `defaultValues`, `lockedFields` array, optional `submitLabel` override. Self-contained validation + submit. ~280 lines. |
| **discard-button.tsx** | Trashed-only UI for draft rows on dashboard. Confirms via `window.confirm()`, calls `discardDraft()`, refreshes page via `router.refresh()`. Shows error inline. |
| **dashboard-skeleton.tsx**, **wizard-skeleton.tsx** | Suspense loader skeletons for streaming RSC boundaries. |

---

### 4. Server-Side Implementation

**Database schema (`/lib/db/schema/extension.ts`):**

| Table | Key Columns | Publish-Relevant |
|-------|-------------|-----------------|
| `extensions` | id (PK), slug (unique), name, nameZh, visibility (enum: draft/published/archived), publisherUserId (FK→users), ownerOrgId (FK→orgs), deptId (FK→depts), category, scope, funcCat, subCat, l2, tagline, description, descriptionZh, homepageUrl, repoUrl, licenseSpdx | All core fields written by `createDraftExtension()` and `updateDraftExtension()` |
| `extensionVersions` | id (PK), extensionId (FK), version, status (enum: pending/scanning/ready/rejected), bundleFileId (logical ref, no FK), publishedAt | Status drives dashboard state machine. `bundleFileId` links to `files.id`. Unique(extensionId, version) prevents duplicate versions. |
| `files` | id (PK), r2Key, size, checksumSha256, mimeType, scanStatus (enum: pending/clean/flagged), scanReport (jsonb) | `scanReport` holds `{ manifestOk, reason, checksum, scannedAt }` from Inngest scan job. Dashboard joins via `extensionVersions.bundleFileId`. |
| `extensionTags` | extensionId (FK), tagId (FK), PK(both) | Many-to-many. Wizard form caps at 8 tags; `updateDraftExtension` deletes + re-inserts wholesale. |

**Row-state machine (`/lib/publish/row-action.ts`)**

Maps (versionStatus, bundleUploaded) → action enum for dashboard rendering:
- `pending` + no bundle → `resume_upload` (clickable, lands on Step 2)
- `pending` + bundle → `resume_submit` (clickable, lands on Step 3)
- `scanning` → `scanning` (non-clickable)
- `ready` → `ready` (non-clickable, awaiting manual publish)
- `rejected` → `rejected` (non-clickable, but rejection reason now shown)
- null/unknown → `none`

**Scan job (`/lib/jobs/scan-bundle.ts`)**

Inngest function (`scan-bundle`, triggered by `extension/scan.requested` event):
1. Fetches bundle from R2 via presigned GET URL
2. Computes SHA-256 checksum
3. Unzips and extracts `manifest.toml`
4. Parses TOML and validates against `BundleManifestSchema` (not the form schema — bundle omits deptId, tags, URLs)
5. Records result via `recordScanResult()`:
   - If valid: sets `files.scanStatus = "clean"`, `extensionVersions.status = "ready"`
   - If invalid: sets `files.scanStatus = "flagged"`, `extensionVersions.status = "rejected"`, reason in `files.scanReport.reason`
6. On success, emits `extension/index.requested` for search indexing

**Error classification (`/lib/actions/publish-errors.ts`)**

Wraps Drizzle/node-postgres exceptions and maps to stable codes:
- `23505` unique_violation → `slug_taken` or `version_taken`
- `23503` foreign_key_violation → `invalid_dept`, `invalid_tag`, `org_missing`, or `invalid_reference`
- `23502` not_null_violation → `missing_required`

**State transitions (`/lib/extensions/state.ts:submit()`, `recordScanResult()`)**

- `submit()`: pending or scanning → scanning (idempotent, for retries)
- `recordScanResult()`: scanning → ready or rejected
- Throws `VersionStateError` if row missing or not in expected state (Inngest catches, no-ops)

**Dashboard rejection display**

Recent commit `213d953`:
- `getMyExtensions()` now joins `files` via logical `extensionVersions.bundleFileId` to fetch `scanReport`
- `extractScanReason()` defensively extracts `report.reason` string (returns null if missing/malformed)
- Dashboard page: when row action is `rejected` and reason present, renders inline below the row in destructive red
- i18n key: `publish.dashboard.rejectedReasonLabel`

---

### 5. Server Actions & Route Handlers (with exports)

**Server Actions (`/lib/actions/publish.ts`, all "use server"):**

| Export | Signature | Purpose |
|--------|-----------|---------|
| `createDraftExtension` | `(raw: ManifestFormValues) → Promise<CreateDraftResult>` | Step 1 submit: create extension + version, validate schema, generate UUIDs. Owner: session.user.id |
| `updateDraftExtension` | `(extensionId: string, raw: ManifestFormValues) → Promise<UpdateDraftResult>` | Edit existing draft. Refuses non-pending versions. Slug/version NOT written (security). Owner-checked. |
| `attachFile` | `(versionId: string, r2Key: string, size: number, checksumSha256: string) → Promise<AttachFileResult>` | Step 2: record uploaded bundle. Creates `files` row, updates `extensionVersions.bundleFileId`. |
| `submitForReview` | `(versionId: string) → Promise<SubmitResult>` | Step 3: transition to scanning, send Inngest event, rollback to pending if event send fails. |
| `discardDraft` | `(extensionId: string) → Promise<DiscardDraftResult>` | Delete draft (visibility == "draft" only). Owner-checked. R2 objects left for bucket lifecycle. |
| `getDraft` | `(extensionId: string) → Promise<GetDraftResult>` | Load draft for resume/edit page. Returns full form values + bundle upload state. Owner-checked. |
| `getMyExtensions` | `(userId: string) → Promise<[Extension & { latestScanReason }]>` | Dashboard: all extensions, latest version + scan reason per extension. |
| `getVersionStatus` | `(versionId: string) → Promise<string \| null>` | Simple status lookup. |

**Route Handlers:**

| Route | Method | Purpose |
|-------|--------|---------|
| `/api/upload/sign` | POST | Generate R2 presigned PUT URL. Accepts slug, version, contentType, size. Returns `{ uploadUrl, r2Key }`. Auth via Bearer token (future CLI) or session cookie. |

**Inngest Functions (`/lib/jobs/index.ts` exports):**

| Export | Trigger | Purpose |
|--------|---------|---------|
| `scanBundle` | `extension/scan.requested` | Download from R2, checksum, validate manifest, record result. |
| `reindexSearch` | `extension/index.requested` | (Not detailed in this mapping; search-related) |

---

### 6. i18n Keys (`messages/en.json` & `messages/zh.json`)

**Publish namespace: `publish.*`**

| Key Path | Usage |
|----------|-------|
| `publish.dashboard.title` | "My Extensions" |
| `publish.dashboard.statusDraft`, `.statusPublished`, `.statusArchived` | Extension visibility badges |
| `publish.dashboard.versionPendingUpload`, `.versionReadyToSubmit`, `.versionScanning`, `.versionReady`, `.versionRejected` | Status labels |
| `publish.dashboard.rejectedReasonLabel` | "Reason" (NEW in commit 213d953) |
| `publish.dashboard.empty`, `.emptyTitle`, `.emptyCta`, `.newButton` | Empty state + CTA |
| `publish.wizard.step1Title`, `.step2Title`, `.step3Title` | Step headers |
| `publish.wizard.nextButton`, `.backButton`, `.submitButton`, `.submitting` | Button labels + loading |
| `publish.wizard.successTitle`, `.successBody`, `.backToDashboard`, `.resumingLabel`, `.saveAndContinue` | Success page, resume banner |
| `publish.form.slug`, `.name`, `.nameZh`, `.version`, `.category`, `.scope`, `.funcCat`, `.subCat`, `.l2`, `.dept`, `.tags`, `.description`, `.descriptionZh`, `.tagline`, `.homepageUrl`, `.repoUrl`, `.licenseSpdx`, `.bundle` | Form field labels |
| `publish.form.slugHint`, `.versionHint`, `.tagsHint`, `.lockedHint` | Helper text |
| `publish.upload.dropzone`, `.maxSize`, `.uploading`, `.uploaded` | Upload zone |
| `publish.upload.errorSize`, `.errorType` | File validation errors |
| `publish.errors.unauthenticated`, `.invalid_input`, `.slug_taken`, `.version_taken`, `.unique_conflict`, `.invalid_dept`, `.invalid_tag`, `.org_missing`, `.invalid_reference`, `.missing_required`, `.no_bundle`, `.version_not_editable`, `.version_not_submittable`, `.scan_queue_unavailable`, `.db_error`, `.fallback` | Server action errors |
| `publish.errors.uploadSign`, `.uploadFailed` | R2 upload errors |
| `publish.discard.ariaLabel`, `.confirm`, `.error_not_found`, `.error_not_discardable`, `.error_unauthenticated`, `.error_fallback` | Discard button + confirm dialog |

---

### 7. Tests

| File | Coverage |
|------|----------|
| `/components/publish/manifest-form.test.tsx` | Form validation, field rendering (basic snapshot/behavior) |
| `/lib/publish/row-action.test.ts` | State routing logic: (status, bundleUploaded) → action enum |
| `/lib/publish/scan-report.test.ts` | `extractScanReason()` defensive JSON parsing |
| `/lib/actions/publish-ownership.test.ts` | Security regressions: `getDraft` + `discardDraft` ownership collapse, `updateDraftExtension` slug/version immutability |
| `/lib/actions/publish-errors.test.ts` | (File exists but not read; likely error code mapping) |

None are end-to-end integration tests; DB-touching paths rely on manual testing or integration suite.

---

### 8. Feature Flags, Environment Variables & CLAUDE.md Notes

**Environment variables (from `.env.example`):**
- `DATABASE_URL` — Postgres connection (Neon)
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` — Public app URL for i18n link generation
- `R2_ACCOUNT_ID`, `R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, `R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`, `R2_BUCKET` — Cloudflare R2 credentials
- `INNGEST_EVENT_KEY` — Inngest cloud event key (dev: skipped if `NODE_ENV !== "production"`, routes to local dev server at `localhost:8288`)
- `INNGEST_SIGNING_KEY` — Webhook signing (not typically needed in production)

**CLAUDE.md locked decisions affecting publish:**
- Multi-tenant schema, single-tenant UI (v1)
- Department IDs: dotted-path text PKs (e.g., `eng.cloud.infra`)
- Dynamic content i18n: column-per-language (EN + ZH)
- R2 for bundle storage
- Inngest for background jobs
- Better Auth for sessions (cookie-based)

**No explicit feature flags** in the publish code; scan results and submissions are always enabled. Dev/prod distinction: Inngest routes to local dev server if `NODE_ENV !== "production"`.

---

### 9. Dashboard Rejection Reason Surface (Commit 213d953)

**Change summary:** Rejected extensions are no longer dead-ends; the rejection reason is now visible inline on the dashboard.

**Implementation:**
- **Query change:** `getMyExtensions()` joins `files` table via logical `extensionVersions.bundleFileId` reference to fetch `files.scanReport` JSONB column
- **Extractor:** New `extractScanReason()` function in `/lib/publish/scan-report.ts` defensively reads `report.reason` (handles null, malformed JSON, missing keys)
- **Dashboard render:** When row action is `rejected` AND reason is present, renders a second row below the extension entry:
  ```
  border-t border-border px-5 py-3 text-xs text-destructive
  "Reason: {extractedReason}"
  ```
- **i18n:** New key `publish.dashboard.rejectedReasonLabel` ("Reason" in EN, "原因" in ZH)

This prevents users from abandoning rejected drafts without knowing why. The reason comes from `scan-bundle.ts` scan failures (missing manifest, invalid TOML, schema validation errors, etc.) stored in the `scanReport` JSONB column.

---

### Summary Table: Full Surface Area

| Layer | Files | Key Exports/Routes |
|-------|-------|-------------------|
| **Pages** | 3 page files + error/loading | `/publish`, `/publish/new`, `/publish/[id]/edit` |
| **Client Components** | 6 components (upload-wizard, manifest-form, discard-button, skeletons) | UploadWizard (3-step state), ManifestForm, DiscardButton |
| **Server Actions** | publish.ts (1 file) | 8 exported actions: create, update, attach, submit, discard, get, getMyExtensions, getVersionStatus |
| **API Routes** | upload/sign/route.ts | POST /api/upload/sign |
| **Jobs** | scan-bundle.ts + client.ts | scanBundle (Inngest fn) |
| **Storage** | r2.ts | generatePresignedPutUrl(), bundleKey(), generatePresignedGetUrl() |
| **Validators** | manifest.ts | ExtensionManifestCore, ManifestFormSchema, BundleManifestSchema |
| **State Machine** | extensions/state.ts | submit(), recordScanResult(), publishVersion() |
| **Helpers** | row-action.ts, scan-report.ts, publish-errors.ts | rowAction(), extractScanReason(), classifyDraftError() |
| **Tests** | 5 test files | ownership security, state routing, JSON parsing, error classification |
| **i18n** | messages/{en,zh}.json | 40+ keys under `publish.*` namespace |
| **DB Schema** | extension.ts | extensions, extensionVersions, files, extensionTags tables |

This design separates concerns cleanly: UI state lives in React, persistent state in Postgres, async work in Inngest, presigned URLs via R2 API. The wizard is resumable at any step (with form pre-fill from DB), and scan rejection reasons are surfaced inline to unblock user workflows.

- Multi-step form flow (Details → Files → Preview → Submit)
- Field inventory (title, description, category dropdowns, file uploads)
- Validation rules and error states
- Success/rejection messaging with reasons
- Dashboard listing with status badges
- Dark mode tokens if applicable

Please share the content in a readable format, and I'll provide the exhaustive specification you need for your React/Tailwind rebuild.

content.content:
25	});
26	
27	export const extensionCategoryEnum = pgEnum("extension_category", [
28	  "skills",
29	  "mcp",
30	  "slash",
31	  "plugins",
32	]);
33	
34	export const extensionScopeEnum = pgEnum("extension_scope", [
35	  "personal",
36	  "org",
37	  "enterprise",
38	]);
39	
40	export const extensionBadgeEnum = pgEnum("extension_badge", [
41	  "official",
42	  "popular",
43	  "new",
44	]);
45	
46	export const funcCatEnum = pgEnum("func_cat", [
47	  "workTask",
48	  "business",
49	  "tools",
50	]);
51	
52	export const visibilityEnum = pgEnum("extension_visibility", [
53	  "draft",
54	  "published",
55	  "archived",
56	]);
57	
58	export const versionStatusEnum = pgEnum("version_status", [
59	  "pending",
60	  "scanning",
61	  "ready",
62	  "rejected",
63	]);
64	
65	export const fileScanStatusEnum = pgEnum("file_scan_status", [
66	  "pending",
67	  "clean",
68	  "flagged",
69	]);
70	
71	export const extensions = pgTable(
72	  "extensions",
73	  {
74	    id: text().primaryKey(),
75	    slug: text().notNull().unique(),
76	    category: extensionCategoryEnum().notNull(),
77	    badge: extensionBadgeEnum(),
78	    scope: extensionScopeEnum().notNull(),
79	    funcCat: funcCatEnum().notNull(),
80	    subCat: text().notNull(),
81	    l2: text(),
82	    publisherUserId: text().references(() => users.id, {
83	      onDelete: "set null",
84	    }),
85	    ownerOrgId: text()
86	      .notNull()
87	      .references(() => organizations.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
88	    deptId: text().references(() => departments.id, { onDelete: "set null" }),
89	    iconEmoji: text(),
90	    iconColor: text(),
91	    visibility: visibilityEnum().notNull().default("draft"),
92	    // i18n columns
93	    name: text().notNull(),
94	    nameZh: text(),
95	    tagline: text(),
96	    taglineZh: text(),
97	    description: text(),
98	    descriptionZh: text(),
99	    // Long-form markdown body for the detail page. Not duplicated to ZH yet —
100	    // most extensions will have an English README, with translations layered
101	    // on later if needed.
102	    readmeMd: text(),
103	    // metadata for detail page sidebar
104	    homepageUrl: text(),
105	    repoUrl: text(),
106	    licenseSpdx: text(),
107	    compatibilityJson: jsonb(),
108	    // denormalized counters (updated by jobs)
109	    downloadsCount: integer().notNull().default(0),
110	    starsAvg: numeric({ precision: 2, scale: 1 }).notNull().default("0.0"),
111	    ratingsCount: integer().notNull().default(0),
112	    publishedAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }),
113	    createdAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
114	    updatedAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
115	    searchVector: tsvector("search_vector"),
116	  },
117	  (t) => [
118	    index("idx_ext_category").on(t.category),
119	    index("idx_ext_scope").on(t.scope),
120	    index("idx_ext_func_sub_l2").on(t.funcCat, t.subCat, t.l2),
121	    index("idx_ext_dept_path").using(
122	      "btree",
123	      sql`${t.deptId} text_pattern_ops`,
124	    ),
125	    index("idx_ext_visibility").on(t.visibility),
126	    index("idx_ext_downloads").on(sql`${t.downloadsCount} DESC`),
127	    index("idx_ext_stars").on(sql`${t.starsAvg} DESC`),
128	  ],
129	);
130	
131	export const extensionVersions = pgTable(
132	  "extension_versions",
133	  {
134	    id: text().primaryKey(),
135	    extensionId: text()
136	      .notNull()
137	      .references(() => extensions.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
138	    version: text().notNull(), // semver
139	    changelog: text(),
140	    changelogZh: text(),
141	    manifestJson: jsonb(),
142	    // Logical reference to files.id; no FK constraint to avoid a circular FK
143	    // with files.extensionVersionId. App code keeps these consistent.
144	    bundleFileId: text(),
145	    status: versionStatusEnum().notNull().default("pending"),
146	    publishedAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }),
147	    createdAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
148	  },
149	  (t) => [unique("ext_version_unique").on(t.extensionId, t.version)],
150	);
151	
152	export const files = pgTable("files", {
153	  id: text().primaryKey(),
154	  extensionVersionId: text().references(() => extensionVersions.id, {
155	    onDelete: "set null",
156	  }),
157	  r2Key: text().notNull(),
158	  size: bigint({ mode: "bigint" }).notNull(),
159	  checksumSha256: text().notNull(),
160	  mimeType: text(),
161	  scanStatus: fileScanStatusEnum().notNull().default("pending"),
162	  scanReport: jsonb(),
163	  createdAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
164	});
165	
166	export const tags = pgTable("tags", {
167	  id: text().primaryKey(), // tag key, e.g. "real-time"
168	  labelEn: text().notNull(),
169	  labelZh: text().notNull(),
170	});
171	
172	export const extensionTags = pgTable(
173	  "extension_tags",
174	  {
175	    extensionId: text()
176	      .notNull()
177	      .references(() => extensions.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
178	    tagId: text()
179	      .notNull()
180	      .references(() => tags.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
181	  },
182	  (t) => [
183	    primaryKey({ columns: [t.extensionId, t.tagId] }),
184	    index("idx_extension_tags_tag_ext").on(t.tagId, t.extensionId),

content.content:
532:**Tag bar** (under scope/department row)
536:- One-click **Clear tags** button
540:- Click any chip to add it to the active filter — chips highlight when active across the whole page
546:Tags compose with all other filters (scope, department, category, search) — so you can ask "show me `#beta` skills in my department" by combining them.
558:Now there is a department filter, scope filter, tags filter, and a trending/sort filter. Since there are so many filters above the list, how can I make them more user-friendly and elegant? What do you think?
571:- **Scope** (personal/org/enterprise) — secondary, low-cardinality → could be a small toggle
577:One single horizontal bar with: Department (left, as breadcrumb), search, then tags as overflow chips, with a **"Filters"** button on the right that opens a dropdown for scope/sort/match-mode. Active filters show as removable pills below.
580:Keep one main filter row (search + dept + sort + a "Filters" pill showing active count). Tags collapse into a drawer that slides open on click. Quieter default state.
583:Move department + scope into the left sidebar (where they conceptually belong as navigation context). Keep only tags + sort above the cards. The sidebar already has hierarchy — adding dept there is natural.
635:Now I'll replace the entire filter block (scope+dept row, tag bar, filter row) with a smart filter component that handles all three modes. Let me also add some i18n strings:
679:Now for Mode C, I should also surface department + scope in the sidebar. Let me check the sidebar's structure:
699:Now make TagBar handle `embedded` prop (it can stay as-is — it just gets a slightly different wrapper but I'll skip that — the existing layout works fine). Let me also ensure the `EXTENSIONS` reference is in scope for FilterAreaA/B (used for tag counts inline):
758:| Tag visibility | Top 5 inline | Hidden until clicked | Always visible |
774:I can click A B C and other buttons in the Tweaks window. how to do?
784:**3. Click it ON** — a floating panel will appear (usually bottom-right corner of the page) titled **"Tweaks"**.
793:Click **A**, **B**, or **C** under "Filter style" and the filter layout will swap instantly.
799:sorry, I just typo. I cannot click A B C and other buttons in the Tweaks window. how to do?
803:Got it — let me investigate. Probably a click handler / pointer-events issue:
823:Fixed — the Tweaks controls were wired with a non-existent `k=` shorthand instead of the proper `value` + `onChange` pair. Now you can click **A / B / C**, switch themes, drag sliders, and toggle the featured banner. Try the **Filter style** row in the Tweaks panel to flip between the three layouts on the live page.
913:Actually — let me look at `TweakRadio`'s value handling. When `tweaks.filterMode || "B"` is passed, and the user clicks A/B/C, `setTweak("filterMode", v)` updates state, page rerenders, **also resends the URL param**, but URL is unchanged, so filterMode stays. Fine.
1135:2. **Reload the file** by clicking on `AgentCenter v2.html` in your file browser again.
1177:The most likely cause: in `FilterAreaA`, when I call `setActiveTags` from inline tag click — but that's an event handler, not render. Fine.

content.text: I have full picture now. Let me confirm two scope decisions before writing the plan.

questions.question: The design exposes three Source methods (zip / Git URL / CLI). Zip is wired today; Git would need a real backend fetcher (clone, validate, checksum, scan). What scope do you want for v1 of this redesign?

options.label: Zip + CLI info only
options.description: Build the redesigned 3-tab Source step UI. Zip works end-to-end (current pipeline). CLI tab is informational (commands + copy buttons). Git tab is hidden or marked "coming soon". Smallest delta. (Recommended)

options.label: All three for real
options.description: Also implement the Git fetcher: server-side clone-or-tarball-download from a public GitHub URL, validate manifest, checksum, queue the existing scan job. Significantly more backend work and security surface.

options.label: Zip only, drop tabs
options.description: Skip the Source-method choice UI entirely — keep just the dropzone. Don't ship the tab pattern from the design. Smallest scope but loses the wizard's structure.

questions.question: The design ties auto-publish behavior to scope: Personal goes live immediately on scan-pass; Org/Enterprise stays in "ready" for admin review. Currently every version waits in "ready" for a manual publish. Implement the scope-driven gate?

options.label: Yes, scope-gated auto-publish
options.description: On scan success: scope=personal → set publishedAt + visibility=published immediately. scope=org|enterprise → stay in "ready" awaiting admin. Updates Inngest scan-bundle.ts and the success-page copy. (Recommended)

options.label: Skip for v1
options.description: Keep the existing flow (always "ready" → manual publish). Show the design's review-screen banner and success-page copy as informational only — actual publish still requires manual action. Tighter scope, fewer behavior changes.

questions.question: The design ships three layout variants under a Tweaks-panel switcher (classic / rail / onepage), but defaults to "rail" — the side-rail layout with the live preview pane. Production stance?

options.label: Rail only
options.description: Ship just the side-rail layout (200px step rail · form · 320px live preview). Drop the classic and onepage variants — they were comparison scaffolding, not real features. Keeps the codebase focused. (Recommended)

options.label: All three with switcher
options.description: Port all three layouts and the Tweaks panel toggle, gated behind a query param or dev-only flag. Useful if you want to keep iterating on layout choice.

input.content:
# Publish Redesign — Implementation Plan

## Context

The current publish flow is a 3-step React wizard (Details → Upload → Review) packed into a single dense form. The redesign in `https://api.anthropic.com/v1/design/h/P0uJulJZcll5AP5nivXugw` (extracted to `/tmp/agentcenter-design-v2/agentcenter/project/AgentCenter.html`, lines 2606-3278) reframes publish as a **4-step rail-layout wizard** with a sticky live preview pane, scope-driven auto-publish, and a friendlier field set.

The redesign also drops/replaces fields that are currently captured in the wizard (funcCat, subCat, l2, descriptionZh, homepageUrl, repoUrl, licenseSpdx, taglineZh) in favor of a leaner Basics + Listing pair — those fields move out of the wizard or become server-derived defaults. Schema changes are minimal.

### User decisions (asked before drafting)
1. **Source methods**: Ship Zip end-to-end + CLI tab as informational copy. Git tab is gated as "coming soon" (UI present, click does nothing).
2. **Auto-publish**: scope=personal → auto-publish on scan success. scope=org|enterprise → stay in `ready` for admin review (current behavior).
3. **Layouts**: Rail-only. Drop the classic and onepage variants from the prototype.

## What changes vs. today

| Area | Current | Target |
|---|---|---|
| Steps | Details → Upload → Review (3) | Basics → Source → Listing → Review (4) |
| Layout | Single column form | 200px step rail · form · 320px live-preview, sticky |
| Source | Zip dropzone | Zip / Git (disabled) / CLI tabs; Zip dropzone + CLI commands w/ copy |
| Type field | `category` select | 4-card grid (Skill · MCP · Slash · Plugin) |
| Scope | Select | 3 stacked choice cards w/ descriptions |
| Tags | Multi-select | Pill input + suggestion chips, `Enter` to add |
| Department | Existing dotted-path picker | Tree dropdown (matches design) |
| Icon | `iconColor` text + `iconEmoji` text | Color swatch picker (5 OKLCH colors) + initial-from-name avatar; emoji input deferred |
| Permissions | Not collected | Checklist: network / files / runtime / data |
| README | Plain textarea | Mono textarea, 8 rows, in Listing step |
| Live preview | None | Card preview + `manifest.json` JSON preview, sticky right rail |
| Auto-publish | All versions wait in `ready` | Personal scope → publish immediately on scan-pass |
| Save Draft | Implicit (every step persists) | Explicit "Save draft" button in header (calls existing update action and exits) |
| ZH inputs | Always shown | Optional toggle "Add Chinese translation" — collapsed by default |

## Implementation strategy — 4 phases, each its own commit

### Phase P1 — schema & server actions

**Files**
- `lib/db/schema/extension.ts` — add columns:
  - `extensions.permissions` `jsonb` default `'{}'` — shape `{network:bool, files:bool, runtime:bool, data:bool}`
  - `extensions.summary` is **not** added; reuse existing `tagline` column for the design's "summary" string (single line, ≤80 chars)
  - `extension_versions.sourceMethod` text, default `'zip'` (no enum yet — leaves room for git/cli without a migration)
  - `extension_versions.sourceMeta` `jsonb` default `'{}'` — placeholder for future git ref/branch
- Make `extensions.funcCat`, `extensions.subCat` **nullable** (drop `notNull` + drizzle default). Existing rows seeded with values stay valid.
- `drizzle/` — generate the migration via `bun run db:generate`. Hand-apply via `db:migrate` per project workflow. Note: this project also needs `db:apply-fts` per memory `project_apply_fts_migration` — keep that step in the verification section.
- `lib/validators/manifest.ts` — extend `ManifestFormSchema`:
  - Add `summary` (max 80, required) — UI maps to `tagline` column
  - Add `permissions` shape (record of bool, optional, default `{}`)
  - Add `sourceMethod` literal `"zip"` (only zip supported in v1)
  - Drop `funcCat`, `subCat`, `l2`, `homepageUrl`, `repoUrl`, `licenseSpdx`, `taglineZh`, `descriptionZh` from the schema (they stay as DB columns, just not collected in v1 wizard)
  - Keep `nameZh`, optional
- `lib/actions/publish.ts`:
  - `createDraftExtension` — when funcCat/subCat are absent, derive defaults from `category`:
    - `skills`/`plugins` → `{funcCat: "tools", subCat: "general"}`
    - `mcp` → `{funcCat: "tools", subCat: "integrations"}`
    - `slash` → `{funcCat: "tools", subCat: "commands"}`
    Encapsulate in a small `defaultClassification(category)` helper near the top of the action file.
  - `updateDraftExtension` — accept the new shape; `permissions` writes to the new column.
  - **No new server action needed** for source method — it's stored as part of the draft.

### Phase P2 — UI: rail layout + 4 steps

Replace `components/publish/upload-wizard.tsx` content (rename internally, keep export name `UploadWizard` so the existing pages don't churn). Old `manifest-form.tsx` is deleted in favor of inline step components — its tests come along (see P4).

**New files**
- `components/publish/wizard/index.tsx` — outer rail layout (200px / 1fr / 320px grid), header with back link + "Save draft", footer with Back/Next/Submit, success page. Manages step state (0..3, 4 = success), step validity, draft persistence on Next, scope-aware success copy.
- `components/publish/wizard/steps/basics.tsx` — fields: name, slug (auto-derived from name unless user overrode), summary (`tagline` column), type (4-card grid), scope (3 stacked cards), and an optional collapsible "Add Chinese translation" panel for `nameZh` + `taglineZh`.
- `components/publish/wizard/steps/source.tsx` — three method tabs:
  - `zip`: existing presigned-PUT flow (wire to existing `/api/upload/sign` + `attachFile` action), drag-drop UI from design (lines 2738-2761)
  - `git`: render the form fields disabled + a small "Coming soon" pill in the header; do not POST
  - `cli`: 3 stacked code blocks with copy buttons (design lines 2774-2797)
- `components/publish/wizard/steps/listing.tsx` — icon color swatches (5 OKLCH preset list, see design line 2608-2614), tag pill input + suggested-tag chips, department tree dropdown, README textarea (mono, rows=8), permissions checklist.
- `components/publish/wizard/steps/review.tsx` — three summary cards (Basics / Source / Listing) with an `Edit` button on each row that jumps back to that step; auto-publish vs. admin-review banner driven by `scope` (design lines 2973-2984); Publisher Terms link.
- `components/publish/wizard/live-preview.tsx` — sticky right pane: card preview (mirrors `ExtCard` layout) + `manifest.json` JSON preview using `JSON.stringify(..., null, 2)`. Pure derivation from current draft state.
- `components/publish/wizard/shared.tsx` — small primitives `PubField`, `PubInput`, `PubChoice` ported from design lines 2616-2664.

**Behavior notes**
- Step rail buttons are clickable for completed steps only (matches `i <= step` gate in design line 3134).
- "Save draft" persists current draft via `updateDraftExtension` then redirects to `/publish` dashboard.
- Invalid Next is disabled (matches `stepsValid[step]` gate, design lines 3053-3059).
- Slug auto-derive uses `slugFromName` (design line 2669): lowercase, hyphenated, max 40 chars, only re-derives if the user hasn't manually edited the slug field.
- Resume mode (`/publish/[id]/edit`) works the same way: load draft via `getDraft`, populate state, lock slug + version on Basics step (matches today's lock UX).

**Pages**
- `app/[locale]/publish/new/page.tsx`, `app/[locale]/publish/[id]/edit/page.tsx` — keep their RSC wrappers; just swap the `<UploadWizard ... />` import target. Same props.
- `app/[locale]/publish/page.tsx` (dashboard) — minor copy tweaks only; rejection-reason row stays as-is.

### Phase P3 — auto-publish for Personal scope

**Files**
- `lib/extensions/state.ts` — extend `recordScanResult(versionId, ok, reason?)`:
  - On `ok=true`, look up `extensions.scope` for the parent extension.
  - If `scope === "personal"`: in the same DB transaction, set `extensionVersions.status = "ready"`, `extensionVersions.publishedAt = now()`, and `extensions.visibility = "published"`. Add a new exported helper `autoPublishIfPersonal(tx, extensionId, versionId)` so the logic is one-liner-callable and testable.
  - If `scope ∈ {"org","enterprise"}`: existing behavior — set `status = "ready"`, leave `publishedAt` null and visibility `draft`.
- `lib/jobs/scan-bundle.ts` — no changes needed; it already calls `recordScanResult`.
- `messages/en.json`, `messages/zh.json` — copy for the success page already lives under `publish.wizard.success*`. Add scope-specific keys:
  - `publish.wizard.successAutoTitle` / `successAutoBody` ("Your extension is live.")
  - `publish.wizard.successReviewTitle` / `successReviewBody` ("We'll email you when admin review is complete.")
  - Pick which to render based on `scope` of the just-submitted version (read from `getVersionStatus` enriched response, or pass the scope down from the wizard state since we already have it client-side).

### Phase P4 — i18n, tests, docs

**i18n** — add ~50 keys under `publish.wizard.*`, `publish.basics.*`, `publish.source.*`, `publish.listing.*`, `publish.review.*`. Mirror the strings in `messages/zh.json` using the design's existing ZH translations (lines 267-505 of the design HTML). Keep existing `publish.errors.*`, `publish.dashboard.*` namespaces intact.

**Tests**
- `components/publish/wizard/steps/basics.test.tsx` — slug auto-derivation, slug user-override sticks, ZH-toggle reveals nameZh/taglineZh.
- `components/publish/wizard/steps/listing.test.tsx` — tag pill add/remove via Enter + Backspace, suggested-tag clicks, permissions toggle.
- `components/publish/wizard/live-preview.test.tsx` — manifest derives from draft; updates as draft changes.
- `lib/extensions/state.test.ts` (extend or create) — `autoPublishIfPersonal`: personal scope sets visibility=published + publishedAt; org/enterprise leaves them; idempotent on retry.
- Keep `lib/actions/publish-ownership.test.ts` and `lib/publish/row-action.test.ts` — both still apply.
- Delete `components/publish/manifest-form.test.tsx` (the component is gone).

**Docs** — `docs/plan.md` — update the Publish phase row to note the redesign. CLAUDE.md decision #6 (README in side panel) still holds. No ADR needed: this is implementation, not a new locked decision.

## Critical files to modify or create

```
NEW   components/publish/wizard/index.tsx
NEW   components/publish/wizard/shared.tsx
NEW   components/publish/wizard/live-preview.tsx
NEW   components/publish/wizard/steps/basics.tsx
NEW   components/publish/wizard/steps/source.tsx
NEW   components/publish/wizard/steps/listing.tsx
NEW   components/publish/wizard/steps/review.tsx
EDIT  components/publish/upload-wizard.tsx           # re-export from new wizard, or replace contents
EDIT  app/[locale]/publish/new/page.tsx              # import path only
EDIT  app/[locale]/publish/[id]/edit/page.tsx       # import path only
EDIT  lib/db/schema/extension.ts                     # add permissions, sourceMethod, sourceMeta; nullable funcCat/subCat
EDIT  lib/validators/manifest.ts                     # new ManifestFormSchema shape
EDIT  lib/actions/publish.ts                         # defaultClassification helper, persist permissions/sourceMethod
EDIT  lib/extensions/state.ts                        # autoPublishIfPersonal in recordScanResult
EDIT  messages/en.json, messages/zh.json             # new keys
DEL   components/publish/manifest-form.tsx           # absorbed into wizard/steps/basics.tsx + listing.tsx
DEL   components/publish/manifest-form.test.tsx
NEW   drizzle/<n>_publish_redesign.sql               # generated migration
```

## Reused functions / utilities

- `lib/storage/r2.ts` — `generatePresignedPutUrl()`, `bundleKey(slug, version)` (zip upload unchanged)
- `lib/actions/publish.ts` — keep existing exports `createDraftExtension`, `updateDraftExtension`, `attachFile`, `submitForReview`, `discardDraft`, `getDraft`, `getMyExtensions`, `getVersionStatus`. All still used.
- `lib/publish/row-action.ts` — `rowAction()` unchanged; dashboard still computes the per-row CTA from (status, bundleUploaded).
- `lib/publish/scan-report.ts` — `extractScanReason()` unchanged; rejection-reason display already shipped (commit `213d953`).
- `lib/jobs/scan-bundle.ts` — unchanged; auto-publish slot in via `recordScanResult`.
- `lib/extensions/state.ts` — `submit()` unchanged; only `recordScanResult()` extended.
- `app/api/upload/sign/route.ts` — unchanged.
- Existing zip drag-drop / size validation logic in current `upload-wizard.tsx` — port the validator code (50MB cap, mime check) into the new `steps/source.tsx` instead of rewriting.

## Verification

End-to-end after implementation, in order:

1. **Schema**: `bun run db:generate && bun run db:migrate && bun run db:apply-fts`. Confirm `permissions`, `source_method`, `source_meta` columns exist; `func_cat`, `sub_cat` are now nullable.
2. **Lint + typecheck**: `bun run lint && bun run typecheck` (project commands). Both clean.
3. **Tests**: `bun test`. New step/preview/state tests pass; old manifest-form test is gone.
4. **Local dev**: start `bun dev` *and* the Inngest dev server (per memory `project_inngest_dev_server`: `npx inngest-cli@latest dev`). Then in a browser:
   - Sign in, hit `/en/publish/new`. Walk all four steps; confirm rail layout, sticky live preview, sticky footer.
   - Try an empty Next click — disabled state.
   - Submit a small zip with a valid `manifest.toml`. Wait for the Inngest scan event. Dashboard should show:
     - Personal scope → `published` visibility + `publishedAt` set, status `ready`.
     - Org scope → status `ready`, visibility still `draft`, awaiting admin.
     - Bad zip → status `rejected`, reason inline (regression check on `extractScanReason`).
   - Hit `/zh/publish/new`. Confirm all new strings translated.
   - Resume an in-progress draft via `/publish/[id]/edit`. Slug + version locked, other fields editable, lands on the right step (per `getDraft` bundle state).
   - Click "Save draft" mid-flow → bounced to dashboard, draft visible.
5. **Visual diff** vs. design: open `/tmp/agentcenter-design-v2/agentcenter/project/AgentCenter.html` in a browser, click "Publish", default `publishMode = "rail"`. Compare rail/preview/spacing side-by-side.

## What this plan deliberately does NOT do

- Git source method (UI shell only, not wired). Tracked as follow-up.
- The classic and onepage layout variants from the prototype.
- Removing `funcCat`/`subCat` columns. They become nullable; admin curation can populate. Filter UI remains compatible because it tolerates nulls (filter only matches non-null rows).
- Icon file upload (the design shows a "Choose file" button). For v1, only color + auto-initial avatar. The `iconEmoji` field stays unused.
- Multi-co-maintainer fields, pricing, runtime compatibility, screenshots — all deferred per the original design chat ("keep minimal and user-friendly").
- Touching `lib/db/seed/**`. Existing seeds keep their funcCat/subCat values.

# Publish Redesign — Implementation Plan

## Context

The current publish flow is a 3-step React wizard (Details → Upload → Review) packed into a single dense form. The redesign also drops/replaces fields that are currently captured in the wizard (funcCat, subCat, l2, descriptionZh, homepageUrl, repoUrl, licenseSpdx, taglineZh) in favor of a leaner Basics + Listing pair — those fields move out of the wizard or become server-derived defaults. Schema changes are minimal.
