input.plan:
# Publish Redesign — Implementation Plan

## Context

The current publish flow is a 3-step React wizard (Details → Upload → Review) packed into a single dense form. The redesign in `https://api.anthropic.com/v1/design/h/P0uJulJZcll5AP5nivXugw` (extracted to `/tmp/agentcenter-design-v2/agentcenter/project/AgentCenter.html`, lines 2606-3278) reframes publish as a **4-step rail-layout wizard** with a sticky live preview pane, scope-driven auto-publish, and a friendlier field set.

The redesign also drops/replaces fields that are currently captured in the wizard (funcCat, subCat, l2, descriptionZh, homepageUrl, repoUrl, licenseSpdx, taglineZh) in favor of a leaner Basics + Listing pair — those fields move out of the wizard or become server-derived defaults. Schema changes are minimal.

### User decisions (asked before drafting)
1. **Source methods**: Ship Zip end-to-end + CLI tab as informational copy. Git tab is gated as "coming soon" (UI present, click does nothing).
2. **Auto-publish**: scope=personal → auto-publish on scan success. scope=org|enterprise → stay in `ready` for admin review (current behavior).
3. **Layouts**: Rail-only. Drop the classic and onepage variants from the prototype.

## What changes vs. today

| Area | Current | Target |
|---|---|---|
| Steps | Details → Upload → Review (3) | Basics → Source → Listing → Review (4) |
| Layout | Single column form | 200px step rail · form · 320px live-preview, sticky |
| Source | Zip dropzone | Zip / Git (disabled) / CLI tabs; Zip dropzone + CLI commands w/ copy |
| Type field | `category` select | 4-card grid (Skill · MCP · Slash · Plugin) |
| Scope | Select | 3 stacked choice cards w/ descriptions |
| Tags | Multi-select | Pill input + suggestion chips, `Enter` to add |
| Department | Existing dotted-path picker | Tree dropdown (matches design) |
| Icon | `iconColor` text + `iconEmoji` text | Color swatch picker (5 OKLCH colors) + initial-from-name avatar; emoji input deferred |
| Permissions | Not collected | Checklist: network / files / runtime / data |
| README | Plain textarea | Mono textarea, 8 rows, in Listing step |
| Live preview | None | Card preview + `manifest.json` JSON preview, sticky right rail |
| Auto-publish | All versions wait in `ready` | Personal scope → publish immediately on scan-pass |
| Save Draft | Implicit (every step persists) | Explicit "Save draft" button in header (calls existing update action and exits) |
| ZH inputs | Always shown | Optional toggle "Add Chinese translation" — collapsed by default |

## Implementation strategy — 4 phases, each its own commit

### Phase P1 — schema & server actions

**Files**
- `lib/db/schema/extension.ts` — add columns:
  - `extensions.permissions` `jsonb` default `'{}'` — shape `{network:bool, files:bool, runtime:bool, data:bool}`
  - `extensions.summary` is **not** added; reuse existing `tagline` column for the design's "summary" string (single line, ≤80 chars)
  - `extension_versions.sourceMethod` text, default `'zip'` (no enum yet — leaves room for git/cli without a migration)
  - `extension_versions.sourceMeta` `jsonb` default `'{}'` — placeholder for future git ref/branch
- Make `extensions.funcCat`, `extensions.subCat` **nullable** (drop `notNull` + drizzle default). Existing rows seeded with values stay valid.
- `drizzle/` — generate the migration via `bun run db:generate`. Hand-apply via `db:migrate` per project workflow. Note: this project also needs `db:apply-fts` per memory `project_apply_fts_migration` — keep that step in the verification section.
- `lib/validators/manifest.ts` — extend `ManifestFormSchema`:
  - Add `summary` (max 80, required) — UI maps to `tagline` column
  - Add `permissions` shape (record of bool, optional, default `{}`)
  - Add `sourceMethod` literal `"zip"` (only zip supported in v1)
  - Drop `funcCat`, `subCat`, `l2`, `homepageUrl`, `repoUrl`, `licenseSpdx`, `taglineZh`, `descriptionZh` from the schema (they stay as DB columns, just not collected in v1 wizard)
  - Keep `nameZh`, optional
- `lib/actions/publish.ts`:
  - `createDraftExtension` — when funcCat/subCat are absent, derive defaults from `category`:
    - `skills`/`plugins` → `{funcCat: "tools", subCat: "general"}`
    - `mcp` → `{funcCat: "tools", subCat: "integrations"}`
    - `slash` → `{funcCat: "tools", subCat: "commands"}`
    Encapsulate in a small `defaultClassification(category)` helper near the top of the action file.
  - `updateDraftExtension` — accept the new shape; `permissions` writes to the new column.
  - **No new server action needed** for source method — it's stored as part of the draft.

### Phase P2 — UI: rail layout + 4 steps

Replace `components/publish/upload-wizard.tsx` content (rename internally, keep export name `UploadWizard` so the existing pages don't churn). Old `manifest-form.tsx` is deleted in favor of inline step components — its tests come along (see P4).

**New files**
- `components/publish/wizard/index.tsx` — outer rail layout (200px / 1fr / 320px grid), header with back link + "Save draft", footer with Back/Next/Submit, success page. Manages step state (0..3, 4 = success), step validity, draft persistence on Next, scope-aware success copy.
- `components/publish/wizard/steps/basics.tsx` — fields: name, slug (auto-derived from name unless user overrode), summary (`tagline` column), type (4-card grid), scope (3 stacked cards), and an optional collapsible "Add Chinese translation" panel for `nameZh` + `taglineZh`.
- `components/publish/wizard/steps/source.tsx` — three method tabs:
  - `zip`: existing presigned-PUT flow (wire to existing `/api/upload/sign` + `attachFile` action), drag-drop UI from design (lines 2738-2761)
  - `git`: render the form fields disabled + a small "Coming soon" pill in the header; do not POST
  - `cli`: 3 stacked code blocks with copy buttons (design lines 2774-2797)
- `components/publish/wizard/steps/listing.tsx` — icon color swatches (5 OKLCH preset list, see design line 2608-2614), tag pill input + suggested-tag chips, department tree dropdown, README textarea (mono, rows=8), permissions checklist.
- `components/publish/wizard/steps/review.tsx` — three summary cards (Basics / Source / Listing) with an `Edit` button on each row that jumps back to that step; auto-publish vs. admin-review banner driven by `scope` (design lines 2973-2984); Publisher Terms link.
- `components/publish/wizard/live-preview.tsx` — sticky right pane: card preview (mirrors `ExtCard` layout) + `manifest.json` JSON preview using `JSON.stringify(..., null, 2)`. Pure derivation from current draft state.
- `components/publish/wizard/shared.tsx` — small primitives `PubField`, `PubInput`, `PubChoice` ported from design lines 2616-2664.

**Behavior notes**
- Step rail buttons are clickable for completed steps only (matches `i <= step` gate in design line 3134).
- "Save draft" persists current draft via `updateDraftExtension` then redirects to `/publish` dashboard.
- Invalid Next is disabled (matches `stepsValid[step]` gate, design lines 3053-3059).
- Slug auto-derive uses `slugFromName` (design line 2669): lowercase, hyphenated, max 40 chars, only re-derives if the user hasn't manually edited the slug field.
- Resume mode (`/publish/[id]/edit`) works the same way: load draft via `getDraft`, populate state, lock slug + version on Basics step (matches today's lock UX).

**Pages**
- `app/[locale]/publish/new/page.tsx`, `app/[locale]/publish/[id]/edit/page.tsx` — keep their RSC wrappers; just swap the `<UploadWizard ... />` import target. Same props.
- `app/[locale]/publish/page.tsx` (dashboard) — minor copy tweaks only; rejection-reason row stays as-is.

### Phase P3 — auto-publish for Personal scope

**Files**
- `lib/extensions/state.ts` — extend `recordScanResult(versionId, ok, reason?)`:
  - On `ok=true`, look up `extensions.scope` for the parent extension.
  - If `scope === "personal"`: in the same DB transaction, set `extensionVersions.status = "ready"`, `extensionVersions.publishedAt = now()`, and `extensions.visibility = "published"`. Add a new exported helper `autoPublishIfPersonal(tx, extensionId, versionId)` so the logic is one-liner-callable and testable.
  - If `scope ∈ {"org","enterprise"}`: existing behavior — set `status = "ready"`, leave `publishedAt` null and visibility `draft`.
- `lib/jobs/scan-bundle.ts` — no changes needed; it already calls `recordScanResult`.
- `messages/en.json`, `messages/zh.json` — copy for the success page already lives under `publish.wizard.success*`. Add scope-specific keys:
  - `publish.wizard.successAutoTitle` / `successAutoBody` ("Your extension is live.")
  - `publish.wizard.successReviewTitle` / `successReviewBody` ("We'll email you when admin review is complete.")
  - Pick which to render based on `scope` of the just-submitted version (read from `getVersionStatus` enriched response, or pass the scope down from the wizard state since we already have it client-side).

### Phase P4 — i18n, tests, docs

**i18n** — add ~50 keys under `publish.wizard.*`, `publish.basics.*`, `publish.source.*`, `publish.listing.*`, `publish.review.*`. Mirror the strings in `messages/zh.json` using the design's existing ZH translations (lines 267-505 of the design HTML). Keep existing `publish.errors.*`, `publish.dashboard.*` namespaces intact.

**Tests**
- `components/publish/wizard/steps/basics.test.tsx` — slug auto-derivation, slug user-override sticks, ZH-toggle reveals nameZh/taglineZh.
- `components/publish/wizard/steps/listing.test.tsx` — tag pill add/remove via Enter + Backspace, suggested-tag clicks, permissions toggle.
- `components/publish/wizard/live-preview.test.tsx` — manifest derives from draft; updates as draft changes.
- `lib/extensions/state.test.ts` (extend or create) — `autoPublishIfPersonal`: personal scope sets visibility=published + publishedAt; org/enterprise leaves them; idempotent on retry.
- Keep `lib/actions/publish-ownership.test.ts` and `lib/publish/row-action.test.ts` — both still apply.
- Delete `components/publish/manifest-form.test.tsx` (the component is gone).

**Docs** — `docs/plan.md` — update the Publish phase row to note the redesign. CLAUDE.md decision #6 (README in side panel) still holds. No ADR needed: this is implementation, not a new locked decision.

## Critical files to modify or create

```
NEW   components/publish/wizard/index.tsx
NEW   components/publish/wizard/shared.tsx
NEW   components/publish/wizard/live-preview.tsx
NEW   components/publish/wizard/steps/basics.tsx
NEW   components/publish/wizard/steps/source.tsx
NEW   components/publish/wizard/steps/listing.tsx
NEW   components/publish/wizard/steps/review.tsx
EDIT  components/publish/upload-wizard.tsx           # re-export from new wizard, or replace contents
EDIT  app/[locale]/publish/new/page.tsx              # import path only
EDIT  app/[locale]/publish/[id]/edit/page.tsx       # import path only
EDIT  lib/db/schema/extension.ts                     # add permissions, sourceMethod, sourceMeta; nullable funcCat/subCat
EDIT  lib/validators/manifest.ts                     # new ManifestFormSchema shape
EDIT  lib/actions/publish.ts                         # defaultClassification helper, persist permissions/sourceMethod
EDIT  lib/extensions/state.ts                        # autoPublishIfPersonal in recordScanResult
EDIT  messages/en.json, messages/zh.json             # new keys
DEL   components/publish/manifest-form.tsx           # absorbed into wizard/steps/basics.tsx + listing.tsx
DEL   components/publish/manifest-form.test.tsx
NEW   drizzle/<n>_publish_redesign.sql               # generated migration
```

## Reused functions / utilities

- `lib/storage/r2.ts` — `generatePresignedPutUrl()`, `bundleKey(slug, version)` (zip upload unchanged)
- `lib/actions/publish.ts` — keep existing exports `createDraftExtension`, `updateDraftExtension`, `attachFile`, `submitForReview`, `discardDraft`, `getDraft`, `getMyExtensions`, `getVersionStatus`. All still used.
- `lib/publish/row-action.ts` — `rowAction()` unchanged; dashboard still computes the per-row CTA from (status, bundleUploaded).
- `lib/publish/scan-report.ts` — `extractScanReason()` unchanged; rejection-reason display already shipped (commit `213d953`).
- `lib/jobs/scan-bundle.ts` — unchanged; auto-publish slot in via `recordScanResult`.
- `lib/extensions/state.ts` — `submit()` unchanged; only `recordScanResult()` extended.
- `app/api/upload/sign/route.ts` — unchanged.
- Existing zip drag-drop / size validation logic in current `upload-wizard.tsx` — port the validator code (50MB cap, mime check) into the new `steps/source.tsx` instead of rewriting.

## Verification

End-to-end after implementation, in order:

   - Hit `/zh/publish/new`. Confirm all new strings translated.
   - Resume an in-progress draft via `/publish/[id]/edit`. Slug + version locked, other fields editable, lands on the right step (per `getDraft` bundle state).
   - Click "Save draft" mid-flow → bounced to dashboard, draft visible.
5. **Visual diff** vs. design: open `/tmp/agentcenter-design-v2/agentcenter/project/AgentCenter.html` in a browser, click "Publish", default `publishMode = "rail"`. Compare rail/preview/spacing side-by-side.

## Reused functions / utilities

- `lib/storage/r2.ts` — `generatePresignedPutUrl()`, `bundleKey(slug, version)` (zip upload unchanged)
- `lib/actions/publish.ts` — keep existing exports `createDraftExtension`, `updateDraftExtension`, `attachFile`, `submitForReview`, `discardDraft`, `getDraft`, `getMyExtensions`, `getVersionStatus`. All still used.
- `lib/publish/row-action.ts` — `rowAction()` unchanged; dashboard still computes the per-row CTA from (status, bundleUploaded).
- `lib/publish/scan-report.ts` — `extractScanReason()` unchanged; rejection-reason display already shipped (commit `213d953`).
- `lib/jobs/scan-bundle.ts` — unchanged; auto-publish slot in via `recordScanResult`.
- `lib/extensions/state.ts` — `submit()` unchanged; only `recordScanResult()` extended.
- `app/api/upload/sign/route.ts` — unchanged.
- Existing zip drag-drop / size validation logic in current `upload-wizard.tsx` — port the validator code (50MB cap, mime check) into the new `steps/source.tsx` instead of rewriting.

## Verification

End-to-end after implementation, in order:

1. **Schema**: `bun run db:generate && bun run db:migrate && bun run db:apply-fts`. Confirm `permissions`, `source_method`, `source_meta` columns exist; `func_cat`, `sub_cat` are now nullable.
2. **Lint + typecheck**: `bun run lint && bun run typecheck` (project commands). Both clean.
3. **Tests**: `bun test`. New step/preview/state tests pass; old manifest-form test is gone.
4. **Local dev**: start `bun dev` *and* the Inngest dev server (per memory `project_inngest_dev_server`: `npx inngest-cli@latest dev`). Then in a browser:
   - Sign in, hit `/en/publish/new`. Walk all four steps; confirm rail layout, sticky live preview, sticky footer.
   - Try an empty Next click — disabled state.
   - Submit a small zip with a valid `manifest.toml`. Wait for the Inngest scan event. Dashboard should show:
     - Personal scope → `published` visibility + `publishedAt` set, status `ready`.
     - Org scope → status `ready`, visibility still `draft`, awaiting admin.
     - Bad zip → status `rejected`, reason inline (regression check on `extractScanReason`).
   - Hit `/zh/publish/new`. Confirm all new strings translated.
   - Resume an in-progress draft via `/publish/[id]/edit`. Slug + version locked, other fields editable, lands on the right step (per `getDraft` bundle state).
   - Click "Save draft" mid-flow → bounced to dashboard, draft visible.
5. **Visual diff** vs. design: open `/tmp/agentcenter-design-v2/agentcenter/project/AgentCenter.html` in a browser, click "Publish", default `publishMode = "rail"`. Compare rail/preview/spacing side-by-side.

## What this plan deliberately does NOT do

- Git source method (UI shell only, not wired). Tracked as follow-up.
- The classic and onepage layout variants from the prototype.
- Removing `funcCat`/`subCat` columns. They become nullable; admin curation can populate. Filter UI remains compatible because it tolerates nulls (filter only matches non-null rows).
- Icon file upload (the design shows a "Choose file" button). For v1, only color + auto-initial avatar. The `iconEmoji` field stays unused.
- Multi-co-maintainer fields, pricing, runtime compatibility, screenshots — all deferred per the original design chat ("keep minimal and user-friendly").
- Touching `lib/db/seed/**`. Existing seeds keep their funcCat/subCat values.

toolUseResult.plan:
# Publish Redesign — Implementation Plan

## Context

The current publish flow is a 3-step React wizard (Details → Upload → Review) packed into a single dense form. The redesign in `https://api.anthropic.com/v1/design/h/P0uJulJZcll5AP5nivXugw` (extracted to `/tmp/agentcenter-design-v2/agentcenter/project/AgentCenter.html`, lines 2606-3278) reframes publish as a **4-step rail-layout wizard** with a sticky live preview pane, scope-driven auto-publish, and a friendlier field set.

The redesign also drops/replaces fields that are currently captured in the wizard (funcCat, subCat, l2, descriptionZh, homepageUrl, repoUrl, licenseSpdx, taglineZh) in favor of a leaner Basics + Listing pair — those fields move out of the wizard or become server-derived defaults. Schema changes are minimal.

### User decisions (asked before drafting)
1. **Source methods**: Ship Zip end-to-end + CLI tab as informational copy. Git tab is gated as "coming soon" (UI present, click does nothing).
2. **Auto-publish**: scope=personal → auto-publish on scan success. scope=org|enterprise → stay in `ready` for admin review (current behavior).
3. **Layouts**: Rail-only. Drop the classic and onepage variants from the prototype.

## What changes vs. today

| Area | Current | Target |
|---|---|---|
| Steps | Details → Upload → Review (3) | Basics → Source → Listing → Review (4) |
| Layout | Single column form | 200px step rail · form · 320px live-preview, sticky |
| Source | Zip dropzone | Zip / Git (disabled) / CLI tabs; Zip dropzone + CLI commands w/ copy |
| Type field | `category` select | 4-card grid (Skill · MCP · Slash · Plugin) |
| Scope | Select | 3 stacked choice cards w/ descriptions |
| Tags | Multi-select | Pill input + suggestion chips, `Enter` to add |
| Department | Existing dotted-path picker | Tree dropdown (matches design) |
| Icon | `iconColor` text + `iconEmoji` text | Color swatch picker (5 OKLCH colors) + initial-from-name avatar; emoji input deferred |
| Permissions | Not collected | Checklist: network / files / runtime / data |
| README | Plain textarea | Mono textarea, 8 rows, in Listing step |
| Live preview | None | Card preview + `manifest.json` JSON preview, sticky right rail |
| Auto-publish | All versions wait in `ready` | Personal scope → publish immediately on scan-pass |
| Save Draft | Implicit (every step persists) | Explicit "Save draft" button in header (calls existing update action and exits) |
| ZH inputs | Always shown | Optional toggle "Add Chinese translation" — collapsed by default |

## Implementation strategy — 4 phases, each its own commit

### Phase P1 — schema & server actions

**Files**
- `lib/db/schema/extension.ts` — add columns:
  - `extensions.permissions` `jsonb` default `'{}'` — shape `{network:bool, files:bool, runtime:bool, data:bool}`
  - `extensions.summary` is **not** added; reuse existing `tagline` column for the design's "summary" string (single line, ≤80 chars)
  - `extension_versions.sourceMethod` text, default `'zip'` (no enum yet — leaves room for git/cli without a migration)
  - `extension_versions.sourceMeta` `jsonb` default `'{}'` — placeholder for future git ref/branch
- Make `extensions.funcCat`, `extensions.subCat` **nullable** (drop `notNull` + drizzle default). Existing rows seeded with values stay valid.
- `drizzle/` — generate the migration via `bun run db:generate`. Hand-apply via `db:migrate` per project workflow. Note: this project also needs `db:apply-fts` per memory `project_apply_fts_migration` — keep that step in the verification section.
- `lib/validators/manifest.ts` — extend `ManifestFormSchema`:
  - Add `summary` (max 80, required) — UI maps to `tagline` column
  - Add `permissions` shape (record of bool, optional, default `{}`)
  - Add `sourceMethod` literal `"zip"` (only zip supported in v1)
  - Drop `funcCat`, `subCat`, `l2`, `homepageUrl`, `repoUrl`, `licenseSpdx`, `taglineZh`, `descriptionZh` from the schema (they stay as DB columns, just not collected in v1 wizard)
  - Keep `nameZh`, optional
- `lib/actions/publish.ts`:
  - `createDraftExtension` — when funcCat/subCat are absent, derive defaults from `category`:
    - `skills`/`plugins` → `{funcCat: "tools", subCat: "general"}`
    - `mcp` → `{funcCat: "tools", subCat: "integrations"}`
    - `slash` → `{funcCat: "tools", subCat: "commands"}`
    Encapsulate in a small `defaultClassification(category)` helper near the top of the action file.
  - `updateDraftExtension` — accept the new shape; `permissions` writes to the new column.
  - **No new server action needed** for source method — it's stored as part of the draft.

### Phase P2 — UI: rail layout + 4 steps

Replace `components/publish/upload-wizard.tsx` content (rename internally, keep export name `UploadWizard` so the existing pages don't churn). Old `manifest-form.tsx` is deleted in favor of inline step components — its tests come along (see P4).

**New files**
- `components/publish/wizard/index.tsx` — outer rail layout (200px / 1fr / 320px grid), header with back link + "Save draft", footer with Back/Next/Submit, success page. Manages step state (0..3, 4 = success), step validity, draft persistence on Next, scope-aware success copy.
- `components/publish/wizard/steps/basics.tsx` — fields: name, slug (auto-derived from name unless user overrode), summary (`tagline` column), type (4-card grid), scope (3 stacked cards), and an optional collapsible "Add Chinese translation" panel for `nameZh` + `taglineZh`.
- `components/publish/wizard/steps/source.tsx` — three method tabs:
  - `zip`: existing presigned-PUT flow (wire to existing `/api/upload/sign` + `attachFile` action), drag-drop UI from design (lines 2738-2761)
  - `git`: render the form fields disabled + a small "Coming soon" pill in the header; do not POST
  - `cli`: 3 stacked code blocks with copy buttons (design lines 2774-2797)
- `components/publish/wizard/steps/listing.tsx` — icon color swatches (5 OKLCH preset list, see design line 2608-2614), tag pill input + suggested-tag chips, department tree dropdown, README textarea (mono, rows=8), permissions checklist.
- `components/publish/wizard/steps/review.tsx` — three summary cards (Basics / Source / Listing) with an `Edit` button on each row that jumps back to that step; auto-publish vs. admin-review banner driven by `scope` (design lines 2973-2984); Publisher Terms link.
- `components/publish/wizard/live-preview.tsx` — sticky right pane: card preview (mirrors `ExtCard` layout) + `manifest.json` JSON preview using `JSON.stringify(..., null, 2)`. Pure derivation from current draft state.
- `components/publish/wizard/shared.tsx` — small primitives `PubField`, `PubInput`, `PubChoice` ported from design lines 2616-2664.

**Behavior notes**
- Step rail buttons are clickable for completed steps only (matches `i <= step` gate in design line 3134).
- "Save draft" persists current draft via `updateDraftExtension` then redirects to `/publish` dashboard.
- Invalid Next is disabled (matches `stepsValid[step]` gate, design lines 3053-3059).
- Slug auto-derive uses `slugFromName` (design line 2669): lowercase, hyphenated, max 40 chars, only re-derives if the user hasn't manually edited the slug field.
- Resume mode (`/publish/[id]/edit`) works the same way: load draft via `getDraft`, populate state, lock slug + version on Basics step (matches today's lock UX).

**Pages**
- `app/[locale]/publish/new/page.tsx`, `app/[locale]/publish/[id]/edit/page.tsx` — keep their RSC wrappers; just swap the `<UploadWizard ... />` import target. Same props.
- `app/[locale]/publish/page.tsx` (dashboard) — minor copy tweaks only; rejection-reason row stays as-is.

### Phase P3 — auto-publish for Personal scope

**Files**
- `lib/extensions/state.ts` — extend `recordScanResult(versionId, ok, reason?)`:
  - On `ok=true`, look up `extensions.scope` for the parent extension.
  - If `scope === "personal"`: in the same DB transaction, set `extensionVersions.status = "ready"`, `extensionVersions.publishedAt = now()`, and `extensions.visibility = "published"`. Add a new exported helper `autoPublishIfPersonal(tx, extensionId, versionId)` so the logic is one-liner-callable and testable.
  - If `scope ∈ {"org","enterprise"}`: existing behavior — set `status = "ready"`, leave `publishedAt` null and visibility `draft`.
- `lib/jobs/scan-bundle.ts` — no changes needed; it already calls `recordScanResult`.
- `messages/en.json`, `messages/zh.json` — copy for the success page already lives under `publish.wizard.success*`. Add scope-specific keys:
  - `publish.wizard.successAutoTitle` / `successAutoBody` ("Your extension is live.")
  - `publish.wizard.successReviewTitle` / `successReviewBody` ("We'll email you when admin review is complete.")
  - Pick which to render based on `scope` of the just-submitted version (read from `getVersionStatus` enriched response, or pass the scope down from the wizard state since we already have it client-side).

### Phase P4 — i18n, tests, docs

**i18n** — add ~50 keys under `publish.wizard.*`, `publish.basics.*`, `publish.source.*`, `publish.listing.*`, `publish.review.*`. Mirror the strings in `messages/zh.json` using the design's existing ZH translations (lines 267-505 of the design HTML). Keep existing `publish.errors.*`, `publish.dashboard.*` namespaces intact.

**Tests**
- `components/publish/wizard/steps/basics.test.tsx` — slug auto-derivation, slug user-override sticks, ZH-toggle reveals nameZh/taglineZh.
- `components/publish/wizard/steps/listing.test.tsx` — tag pill add/remove via Enter + Backspace, suggested-tag clicks, permissions toggle.
- `components/publish/wizard/live-preview.test.tsx` — manifest derives from draft; updates as draft changes.
- `lib/extensions/state.test.ts` (extend or create) — `autoPublishIfPersonal`: personal scope sets visibility=published + publishedAt; org/enterprise leaves them; idempotent on retry.
- Keep `lib/actions/publish-ownership.test.ts` and `lib/publish/row-action.test.ts` — both still apply.
- Delete `components/publish/manifest-form.test.tsx` (the component is gone).

**Docs** — `docs/plan.md` — update the Publish phase row to note the redesign. CLAUDE.md decision #6 (README in side panel) still holds. No ADR needed: this is implementation, not a new locked decision.

## Critical files to modify or create

```
NEW   components/publish/wizard/index.tsx
NEW   components/publish/wizard/shared.tsx
NEW   components/publish/wizard/live-preview.tsx
NEW   components/publish/wizard/steps/basics.tsx

NEW   components/publish/wizard/steps/review.tsx
EDIT  components/publish/upload-wizard.tsx           # re-export from new wizard, or replace contents
EDIT  app/[locale]/publish/new/page.tsx              # import path only
EDIT  app/[locale]/publish/[id]/edit/page.tsx       # import path only
EDIT  lib/db/schema/extension.ts                     # add permissions, sourceMethod, sourceMeta; nullable funcCat/subCat
EDIT  lib/validators/manifest.ts                     # new ManifestFormSchema shape
EDIT  lib/actions/publish.ts                         # defaultClassification helper, persist permissions/sourceMethod
EDIT  lib/extensions/state.ts                        # autoPublishIfPersonal in recordScanResult
EDIT  messages/en.json, messages/zh.json             # new keys
DEL   components/publish/manifest-form.tsx           # absorbed into wizard/steps/basics.tsx + listing.tsx
DEL   components/publish/manifest-form.test.tsx
NEW   drizzle/<n>_publish_redesign.sql               # generated migration
```

- Existing zip drag-drop / size validation logic in current `upload-wizard.tsx` — port the validator code (50MB cap, mime check) into the new `steps/source.tsx` instead of rewriting.

## Verification

End-to-end after implementation, in order:

1. **Schema**: `bun run db:generate && bun run db:migrate && bun run db:apply-fts`. Confirm `permissions`, `source_method`, `source_meta` columns exist; `func_cat`, `sub_cat` are now nullable.

content: Auto mode lets Claude handle permission prompts automatically — Claude checks each tool call for risky actions and prompt injection before executing. Actions Claude identifies as safe are executed, while actions Claude identifies as risky are blocked and Claude may try a different approach. Ideal for long-running tasks. Sessions are slightly more expensive. Claude can make mistakes that allow harmful commands to run, it's recommended to only use in isolated environments. Shift+Tab to change mode.

1	import { sql } from "drizzle-orm";
2	import {
3	  bigint,
4	  customType,
5	  index,
6	  integer,
7	  jsonb,
8	  numeric,
9	  pgEnum,
10	  pgTable,
11	  primaryKey,
12	  text,
13	  timestamp,
14	  unique,
15	} from "drizzle-orm/pg-core";
16	
17	import { users } from "./auth";
18	import { departments, organizations } from "./org";
19	
20	// tsvector is a Postgres-native type; Drizzle doesn't ship a built-in helper.
21	// We declare it as a custom type so the ORM can reference it in WHERE clauses
22	// via sql``. It is GENERATED ALWAYS (read-only); never written by app code.
23	const tsvector = customType<{ data: string }>({
24	  dataType() { return "tsvector"; },
25	});
26	
27	export const extensionCategoryEnum = pgEnum("extension_category", [
28	  "skills",
29	  "mcp",
30	  "slash",
31	  "plugins",
32	]);
33	
34	export const extensionScopeEnum = pgEnum("extension_scope", [
35	  "personal",
36	  "org",
37	  "enterprise",
38	]);
39	
40	export const extensionBadgeEnum = pgEnum("extension_badge", [
41	  "official",
42	  "popular",
43	  "new",
44	]);
45	
46	export const funcCatEnum = pgEnum("func_cat", [
47	  "workTask",
48	  "business",
49	  "tools",
50	]);
51	
52	export const visibilityEnum = pgEnum("extension_visibility", [
53	  "draft",
54	  "published",
55	  "archived",
56	]);
57	
58	export const versionStatusEnum = pgEnum("version_status", [
59	  "pending",
60	  "scanning",
61	  "ready",
62	  "rejected",
63	]);
64	
65	export const fileScanStatusEnum = pgEnum("file_scan_status", [
66	  "pending",
67	  "clean",
68	  "flagged",
69	]);
70	
71	export const extensions = pgTable(
72	  "extensions",
73	  {
74	    id: text().primaryKey(),
75	    slug: text().notNull().unique(),
76	    category: extensionCategoryEnum().notNull(),
77	    badge: extensionBadgeEnum(),
78	    scope: extensionScopeEnum().notNull(),
79	    funcCat: funcCatEnum().notNull(),
80	    subCat: text().notNull(),
81	    l2: text(),
82	    publisherUserId: text().references(() => users.id, {
83	      onDelete: "set null",
84	    }),
85	    ownerOrgId: text()
86	      .notNull()
87	      .references(() => organizations.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
88	    deptId: text().references(() => departments.id, { onDelete: "set null" }),
89	    iconEmoji: text(),
90	    iconColor: text(),
91	    visibility: visibilityEnum().notNull().default("draft"),
92	    // i18n columns
93	    name: text().notNull(),
94	    nameZh: text(),
95	    tagline: text(),
96	    taglineZh: text(),
97	    description: text(),
98	    descriptionZh: text(),
99	    // Long-form markdown body for the detail page. Not duplicated to ZH yet —
100	    // most extensions will have an English README, with translations layered
101	    // on later if needed.
102	    readmeMd: text(),
103	    // metadata for detail page sidebar
104	    homepageUrl: text(),
105	    repoUrl: text(),
106	    licenseSpdx: text(),
107	    compatibilityJson: jsonb(),
108	    // denormalized counters (updated by jobs)
109	    downloadsCount: integer().notNull().default(0),
110	    starsAvg: numeric({ precision: 2, scale: 1 }).notNull().default("0.0"),
111	    ratingsCount: integer().notNull().default(0),
112	    publishedAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }),
113	    createdAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
114	    updatedAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
115	    searchVector: tsvector("search_vector"),
116	  },
117	  (t) => [
118	    index("idx_ext_category").on(t.category),
119	    index("idx_ext_scope").on(t.scope),
120	    index("idx_ext_func_sub_l2").on(t.funcCat, t.subCat, t.l2),
121	    index("idx_ext_dept_path").using(
122	      "btree",
123	      sql`${t.deptId} text_pattern_ops`,
124	    ),
125	    index("idx_ext_visibility").on(t.visibility),
126	    index("idx_ext_downloads").on(sql`${t.downloadsCount} DESC`),
127	    index("idx_ext_stars").on(sql`${t.starsAvg} DESC`),
128	  ],
129	);
130	
131	export const extensionVersions = pgTable(
132	  "extension_versions",
133	  {

content.text: Now let me look at the existing manifest validators and publish action to refactor them.

content.content:
1	import { z } from "zod";
2	
3	// Slug shape, exported so client-side form validators don't redefine it.
4	export const SLUG_PATTERN = /^[a-z0-9]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*$/;
5	export const SEMVER_PATTERN = /^\d+\.\d+\.\d+$/;
6	
7	// Single source of truth for what makes an extension manifest valid.
8	// Mirrors `docs/manifest-spec.md`. Form and bundle schemas below derive
9	// from this core; nothing should redefine these constraints elsewhere.
10	export const ExtensionManifestCore = z.object({
11	  slug: z
12	    .string()
13	    .min(3)
14	    .max(64)
15	    .regex(SLUG_PATTERN, "Lowercase letters, numbers and hyphens only"),
16	  name: z.string().min(2).max(80),
17	  nameZh: z.string().max(80).optional(),
18	  version: z.string().regex(SEMVER_PATTERN, "Must be semver (e.g. 1.0.0)"),
19	  category: z.enum(["skills", "mcp", "slash", "plugins"]),
20	  scope: z.enum(["personal", "org", "enterprise"]),
21	  funcCat: z.enum(["workTask", "business", "tools"]),
22	  subCat: z.string().min(1).max(60),
23	  l2: z.string().max(60).optional(),
24	  description: z.string().min(1).max(280),
25	  descriptionZh: z.string().max(280).optional(),
26	  tagline: z.string().max(120).optional(),
27	});
28	
29	// Form: every core field plus the UI-only extras the publish wizard collects.
30	export const ManifestFormSchema = ExtensionManifestCore.extend({
31	  tagIds: z.array(z.string()).max(8),
32	  deptId: z.string().optional(),
33	  homepageUrl: z.string().url().optional().or(z.literal("")),
34	  repoUrl: z.string().url().optional().or(z.literal("")),
35	  licenseSpdx: z.string().max(40).optional(),
36	});
37	
38	export type ManifestFormValues = z.infer<typeof ManifestFormSchema>;
39	
40	// Bundle: same fields, lifted into the [extension] / [categorization] TOML
41	// sections defined in docs/manifest-spec.md.
42	export const BundleManifestSchema = z.object({
43	  extension: ExtensionManifestCore.pick({
44	    slug: true,
45	    name: true,
46	    nameZh: true,
47	    version: true,
48	    category: true,
49	    scope: true,
50	    description: true,
51	    descriptionZh: true,
52	    tagline: true,
53	  }),
54	  categorization: ExtensionManifestCore.pick({
55	    funcCat: true,
56	    subCat: true,
57	    l2: true,
58	  }),
59	});
60	
61	export type BundleManifest = z.infer<typeof BundleManifestSchema>;

content.content:
1	import { Button as ButtonPrimitive } from "@base-ui/react/button"
2	import { cva, type VariantProps } from "class-variance-authority"
3	
4	import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"
5	
6	const buttonVariants = cva(
7	  "group/button inline-flex shrink-0 items-center justify-center rounded-lg border border-transparent bg-clip-padding text-sm font-medium whitespace-nowrap transition-all outline-none select-none focus-visible:border-ring focus-visible:ring-3 focus-visible:ring-ring/50 active:not-aria-[haspopup]:translate-y-px disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50 aria-invalid:border-destructive aria-invalid:ring-3 aria-invalid:ring-destructive/20 dark:aria-invalid:border-destructive/50 dark:aria-invalid:ring-destructive/40 [&_svg]:pointer-events-none [&_svg]:shrink-0 [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-4",
8	  {
9	    variants: {
10	      variant: {
11	        default: "bg-primary text-primary-foreground [a]:hover:bg-primary/80",
12	        outline:
13	          "border-border bg-background hover:bg-muted hover:text-foreground aria-expanded:bg-muted aria-expanded:text-foreground dark:border-input dark:bg-input/30 dark:hover:bg-input/50",
14	        secondary:
15	          "bg-secondary text-secondary-foreground hover:bg-secondary/80 aria-expanded:bg-secondary aria-expanded:text-secondary-foreground",
16	        ghost:
17	          "hover:bg-muted hover:text-foreground aria-expanded:bg-muted aria-expanded:text-foreground dark:hover:bg-muted/50",
18	        destructive:
19	          "bg-destructive/10 text-destructive hover:bg-destructive/20 focus-visible:border-destructive/40 focus-visible:ring-destructive/20 dark:bg-destructive/20 dark:hover:bg-destructive/30 dark:focus-visible:ring-destructive/40",
20	        link: "text-primary underline-offset-4 hover:underline",
21	      },
22	      size: {
23	        default:
24	          "h-8 gap-1.5 px-2.5 has-data-[icon=inline-end]:pr-2 has-data-[icon=inline-start]:pl-2",
25	        xs: "h-6 gap-1 rounded-[min(var(--radius-md),10px)] px-2 text-xs in-data-[slot=button-group]:rounded-lg has-data-[icon=inline-end]:pr-1.5 has-data-[icon=inline-start]:pl-1.5 [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-3",
26	        sm: "h-7 gap-1 rounded-[min(var(--radius-md),12px)] px-2.5 text-[0.8rem] in-data-[slot=button-group]:rounded-lg has-data-[icon=inline-end]:pr-1.5 has-data-[icon=inline-start]:pl-1.5 [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-3.5",
27	        lg: "h-9 gap-1.5 px-2.5 has-data-[icon=inline-end]:pr-2 has-data-[icon=inline-start]:pl-2",
28	        icon: "size-8",
29	        "icon-xs":
30	          "size-6 rounded-[min(var(--radius-md),10px)] in-data-[slot=button-group]:rounded-lg [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-3",
31	        "icon-sm":
32	          "size-7 rounded-[min(var(--radius-md),12px)] in-data-[slot=button-group]:rounded-lg",
33	        "icon-lg": "size-9",
34	      },
35	    },
36	    defaultVariants: {
37	      variant: "default",
38	      size: "default",
39	    },
40	  }
41	)
42	
43	function Button({
44	  className,
45	  variant = "default",
46	  size = "default",
47	  ...props
48	}: ButtonPrimitive.Props & VariantProps<typeof buttonVariants>) {
49	  return (
50	    <ButtonPrimitive
51	      data-slot="button"
52	      className={cn(buttonVariants({ variant, size, className }))}
53	      {...props}
54	    />
55	  )
56	}
57	
58	export { Button, buttonVariants }
59

lines: -// Render a thrown client-side error's message under the friendly headline,
-// but only in development. Mirrors the server-side `devErrorDetail` helper.
-// Error codes the server actions can return. Anything else falls through to
-// Subset of `DraftSnapshot` from the resume server action — kept here as a
-// structural type so this component doesn't import server-only modules.
-  // Full form values pre-fill Step 1 in resume mode, so a user can fix
-  // a typo without discarding and recreating the draft.
-  // When set, the wizard runs in "resume" mode: Step 1 is skipped, the
-  // manifest details are shown read-only at the top of every step, and the
-  // initial step is derived from whether the bundle is already uploaded.
-  // Translate a server-action error code into a localized message. Unknown
-  // codes fall back to a generic message rather than leaking raw codes (e.g.
-  // Reset the file-upload state. Called after a fresh draft is created so
-  // a re-edited Step 1 doesn't leave the wizard thinking the previous
-  // bundle is still attached to the new versionId.
-      // Edit mode: update the existing extension + version in place.
-      // Slug and version are locked in the form so the bundle's R2 key
-      // remains valid; we don't reset upload state here.
-        // Mirror the latest values into local draft state so Step 3's
-        // summary panel reflects what the user just saved.
-        body: JSON.stringify({ slug: draft.slug, version: draft.version, contentType: "application/zip", size: file.size }),
-        // Try to read a `detail` from the error body — the route attaches
-        // the underlying message in development to help diagnose missing
-        // R2 config etc. Tolerate non-JSON bodies (e.g. proxy errors).
-          const body = (await signRes.json()) as { detail?: string };
-      const { uploadUrl, r2Key } = await signRes.json() as { uploadUrl: string; r2Key: string };
-      const putRes = await fetch(uploadUrl, { method: "PUT", headers: { "Content-Type": "application/zip" }, body: file });
-      // 3. Record file in DB (checksum placeholder — real checksum done by Inngest scan)
-      const attachResult = await attachFile(draft.versionId, r2Key, file.size, "pending");
-      <div className="flex flex-col items-center gap-4 py-16 text-center">
-      {/* Resume banner: surfaces what we're picking back up so the wizard
-          isn't a context-free three-step UI in the middle of a flow. */}
-        <div className="mb-6 rounded-xl border border-border bg-card px-5 py-4 flex items-center justify-between gap-3">
-            <span className={`flex h-7 w-7 items-center justify-center rounded-full text-xs font-semibold border ${
-        <div className="mb-4 rounded-md border border-red-200 bg-red-50 px-4 py-3 text-sm text-red-700">
-            <pre className="mt-2 whitespace-pre-wrap break-all rounded bg-red-100/70 px-2 py-1 font-mono text-xs text-red-900/80">
-      {/* Step 1: Manifest. In resume mode the form is pre-filled with the
-          existing values and `slug` + `version` are locked (they form
-          the R2 bundle key — letting them change orphans the bundle). */}
-            className="flex flex-col items-center justify-center rounded-xl border-2 border-dashed border-border p-12 text-center cursor-pointer hover:border-primary transition-colors"
-            {/* Step 1 is editable in both new and resume modes now, so

-              className="rounded-md border border-border px-4 py-2 text-sm hover:bg-muted transition-colors"
-              className="rounded-md bg-primary px-5 py-2 text-sm font-medium text-primary-foreground hover:opacity-90 disabled:opacity-40 transition-opacity"
-          <div className="rounded-xl border border-border bg-card p-6 space-y-2 text-sm">

file.content:
```
[Publisher] /publish/new  (3-step wizard, client component)
  Step 1: Manifest form  (name, slug, category, scope, funcCat/subCat/l2, dept,
                          tags, description × 2 locales, install paths per agent)
            → Zod validate → server action createDraftExtension() → returns extensionId
  Step 2: File upload     POST /api/upload/sign  → R2 presigned PUT
                          → browser uploads bundle directly to R2
                          → server action attachFile(extensionId, fileId)
  Step 3: Review & submit → server action submitForReview(extensionId)
                          → submit(versionId) flips status to 'scanning'
                            (idempotent: pending|scanning → scanning, so a retry
                             after a failed inngest.send can re-queue the scan)
                          → inngest.send({ name: 'extension/scan.requested', ... })
                            (best-effort rollback to 'pending' on send failure)
                          → return to /publish dashboard

[Publisher] /publish (dashboard)
  - Lists my drafts with stage (needs upload / ready to submit / scanning / …).
  - Resumable rows (status='pending') link to /publish/[id]/edit → same wizard,
    pre-filled, slug+version locked while a bundle is uploaded (they form the
    R2 bundle key bundles/<slug>/<version>/bundle.zip).
  - Per-row Discard button hard-deletes the draft (FK cascades). R2 bundle
    objects are left for bucket lifecycle.

[Background, Inngest]
  on 'extension/scan.requested':
    1. fetch from R2
    2. checksum + size guard
    3. parse manifest.json against ManifestSchema (Zod)
    4. (optional) clamav scan
    5. write scanReport to files; on clean → status='ready'
    6. inngest.send({ name: 'extension/index.requested', ... })
  on 'extension/index.requested':
    1. UPDATE extensions SET searchVector = ...
    2. flip visibility='published' if all checks pass
    3. revalidateTag('extensions')
    4. inngest.send({ name: 'extension/published', ... })  // for downstream notifications later
```

**Synchronous** (server action, returns < 200ms): manifest creation, presigned URL signing, attach-file, submitForReview enqueue.
**Async** (Inngest): bundle download, scan, validation, indexing, publish flip.

The manifest spec lives in `docs/manifest-spec.md` (drafted in Phase 8 before the wizard). Includes:
- Identity: `slug`, `name`, `nameZh`, `version` (semver), `category`, `license`

input.new_string:
[Publisher] /publish/new  (4-step rail wizard, client component — redesigned)
  Step 1: Basics          (name, slug auto-derived, summary ≤80, type, scope;
                            optional ZH translation panel)
            → Zod validate → server action createDraftExtension() → returns extensionId
            → server defaults backfill funcCat/subCat off `category`
  Step 2: Source          (zip / git / cli tabs — only zip wired in v1)
                          → POST /api/upload/sign  → R2 presigned PUT
                          → browser uploads bundle directly to R2
                          → server action attachFile(extensionId, fileId)
  Step 3: Listing         (icon colour swatch, tag pill input, dept tree
                            dropdown, README markdown, permissions checklist)
            → server action updateDraftExtension() persists incremental edits
  Step 4: Review          → summary cards w/ Edit jump-to-step, scope-aware
                            auto-publish vs. admin-review banner
                          → submitForReview(versionId)
                          → submit(versionId) flips status to 'scanning'
                            (idempotent: pending|scanning → scanning, so a retry
                             after a failed inngest.send can re-queue the scan)
                          → inngest.send({ name: 'extension/scan.requested', ... })
                            (best-effort rollback to 'pending' on send failure)
                          → return to /publish dashboard

content.text:
All four phases done. Per project workflow I'll pause for your go-ahead before committing. Here's the summary of what landed and the proposed commit plan.

## What's in the branch `feat/publish-redesign`

**Phase P1 — schema + validators + actions** (8 files)
- `lib/db/schema/extension.ts`: added `permissions jsonb`, `extension_versions.sourceMethod text`, `sourceMeta jsonb`; made `funcCat`/`subCat` nullable
- `drizzle/0002_thick_madame_hydra.sql` + journal/snapshot — migration ready to apply (search_vector line removed; the FTS column is still hand-applied via `db:apply-fts` per project memory)
- `lib/validators/manifest.ts`: form schema slimmed; `summary` (≤80 chars, maps to `tagline` column), `permissions`, `iconColor`, `sourceMethod` added; URL/license/funcCat fields dropped from form (kept in core for bundle TOML)
- `lib/actions/publish.ts`: `defaultClassification(category)` helper backfills funcCat/subCat; create/update/getDraft now wired to the new shape
- Tests updated: `manifest.test.ts`, `publish-ownership.test.ts`

**Phase P2 — rail wizard UI** (11 files)
- New `components/publish/wizard/`: `index.tsx`, `shared.tsx`, `live-preview.tsx`, `icon-colors.ts`, `steps/{basics,source,listing,review}.tsx` — 200px rail · form · 320px sticky preview, 4 steps + scope-aware success page, save-draft button, slug auto-derivation
- Old `components/publish/manifest-form.tsx` + test deleted
- `components/publish/upload-wizard.tsx` is now a re-export so page imports keep working
- `app/[locale]/publish/{new,[id]/edit}/page.tsx`: simpler wrappers (wizard owns its own header)
- `lib/i18n/messages/{en,zh}.json`: ~50 new keys under `publish.{wizard,basics,source,listing,review,permissions,type,scope,preview,upload}`

**Phase P3 — auto-publish** (2 files)
- `lib/extensions/state.ts`: `recordScanResult` joins extensions to read `scope`; on `ok` + `scope === "personal"` it stamps `extensionVersions.publishedAt` and flips `extensions.visibility = "published"` in the same transaction. Org/Enterprise unchanged.
- `lib/extensions/state.test.ts`: split into org-stays-ready and personal-auto-publishes cases

**Phase P4 — tests + docs** (4 files)
- New tests: `wizard/steps/basics.test.tsx` (slug derivation + ZH toggle), `wizard/steps/listing.test.tsx` (tag pills + permissions), `wizard/live-preview.test.tsx` (manifest derivation)
- `docs/plan.md`: publish-flow section rewritten for the 4-step wizard + auto-publish path

## Verification done

- `bun run validate` (lint + typecheck + 279 tests across 32 files) — clean
- `bun run build` — clean, all `/publish/*` routes registered
- Migration **not applied** to your DB yet — it's reversible-only-on-rollback so I left that to you. Apply with `bun run db:migrate && bun run db:apply-fts` (the latter per memory note for the FTS column on fresh DBs).
- Manual UI walk in a browser still pending — wants Inngest dev server running.

## Proposed commits

1. `feat(publish-p1): nullable funcCat/subCat + add permissions/source columns + slimmed form schema`
2. `feat(publish-p2): redesigned 4-step rail wizard with live preview pane`
3. `feat(publish-p3): auto-publish personal-scope versions on scan success`
4. `docs(publish): update plan.md for redesigned wizard + auto-publish path`

Each phase keeps tests + i18n + code together. Ready to commit when you give the word.

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(publish-p1): nullable funcCat/subCat + add permissions/source columns

The redesigned publish wizard collects fewer fields than the legacy form.
funcCat/subCat are no longer surfaced, so they become nullable; a server
helper backfills them off `category` for new drafts so filter queries
keep matching. New columns capture what the wizard does collect:

- extensions.permissions jsonb: network/files/runtime/data toggles
- extension_versions.sourceMethod text default 'zip': leaves room for
  git/cli without an enum migration when those land
- extension_versions.sourceMeta jsonb: future-proofing for git ref/branch

ManifestFormSchema is restructured around the new shape: `summary`
(maps to existing `tagline` column), `permissions`, `iconColor`,
`sourceMethod`, optional `taglineZh`. URL/license/funcCat/subCat are
gone from the form (still nullable in DB; bundle TOML still requires
description per docs/manifest-spec.md).

Migration drizzle/0002_thick_madame_hydra.sql intentionally drops the
search_vector ADD COLUMN line drizzle introspection re-suggests — that
column is hand-applied via `bun run db:apply-fts`.
EOF
)"

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(publish-p2): redesigned 4-step rail wizard with live preview pane

Replaces the 3-step packed form with the 4-step layout from the
redesign (Basics → Source → Listing → Review). Rail layout: 200px
step rail · form column · 320px sticky live-preview pane that mirrors
the listing card and a derived manifest.json.

Highlights:
- BasicsStep: name with auto-derived slug, type 4-card grid, scope
  3-card stack, optional collapsible ZH translation panel
- SourceStep: zip / git / cli tab choice. Zip uses the existing R2
  presign + attachFile pipeline; git is gated behind a "Soon" badge
  (not wired this PR); cli renders 3 copy-able commands
- ListingStep: 5-color icon swatch with auto-initial avatar, tag
  pill input + suggested-tag chips + Backspace-to-remove, lightweight
  controlled department tree dropdown (separate from the URL-driven
  filter picker), README markdown textarea, permissions checklist
- ReviewStep: summary cards with Edit jump-to-step, scope-aware
  auto-publish vs admin-review banner, terms link
- LivePreview: pure derivation from current draft; renders on every
  step

Save Draft button persists via the existing updateDraftExtension
action and redirects to the dashboard. Resume mode locks slug +
version on Basics so the bundle's R2 key stays valid.

Co-located tests cover slug derivation, ZH toggle, tag pill add/
remove + dedupe, permission toggles, and manifest derivation.

Drops the legacy components/publish/manifest-form.{tsx,test.tsx}.
upload-wizard.tsx becomes a thin re-export so existing imports keep
working without a project-wide rename.

i18n adds ~50 keys under publish.{wizard,basics,source,listing,
review,permissions,type,scope,preview,upload}; legacy publish.form
is dropped.
EOF
)"

input.command:
git add lib/extensions/state.ts lib/extensions/state.test.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(publish-p3): auto-publish personal-scope versions on scan success

Personal-scope drafts skip admin review entirely now. recordScanResult
joins extensions to read the parent scope; on a clean scan it writes:

- scope = personal: status='ready' + publishedAt=now on the version,
  visibility='published' + publishedAt=now on the extension, all in
  the same transaction. The Review-step success page renders the
  "Your extension is live" copy.
- scope = org | enterprise: existing behavior — status='ready', no
  publishedAt, visibility stays 'draft' awaiting admin curation.

Failure path (flagged + rejected) is scope-agnostic and unchanged.

Tests split the recordScanResult coverage into the two scopes plus
the existing missing/terminal-version error paths.
EOF
)"

All four phases committed on `feat/publish-redesign`, tree clean, 279/279 tests pass.

## Branch summary

```
5ed8e27 docs(publish): update plan.md for redesigned wizard + auto-publish path
3ab80ee feat(publish-p3): auto-publish personal-scope versions on scan success
0c18b88 feat(publish-p2): redesigned 4-step rail wizard with live preview pane
9a3b210 feat(publish-p1): nullable funcCat/subCat + add permissions/source columns
```

## Still owed before merge (need your hands)

1. **Apply the migration** — `bun run db:migrate && bun run db:apply-fts`. The schema changes are additive + nullable, so existing rows are safe; rolling back means manually dropping the new columns.
2. **Manual UI walk** — needs Inngest dev (`npx inngest-cli@latest dev`) running locally:
   - `/en/publish/new` → walk all four steps with a small zip; verify rail layout, sticky preview, and that scope=personal flips visibility to `published` after the scan completes
   - `/zh/publish/new` → confirm Chinese strings throughout
   - Resume an in-progress draft via the dashboard
   - Save Draft mid-flow → bounces back to dashboard
3. **Open the PR** when you're happy — `gh pr create` against `main`. CI + CodeRabbit will run.

Want me to push the branch and open the PR now?

input.command:
gh pr create --base main --head feat/publish-redesign --title "feat(publish): redesigned 4-step wizard with rail layout + auto-publish" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Rebuilds the publish flow off the redesigned [Claude Design](https://api.anthropic.com/v1/design/h/P0uJulJZcll5AP5nivXugw?open_file=AgentCenter.html) handoff: 4-step rail wizard with a sticky live-preview pane, scope-driven auto-publish, and a leaner field set.

- **Wizard layout** — 200px step rail · form · 320px live preview. Steps: Basics → Source → Listing → Review. Drops the prototype's classic + onepage variants (rail-only).
- **Field set** — name (with auto-derived slug), summary (≤80, maps to existing `tagline` column), type 4-card grid, scope 3-card stack, optional ZH translation panel. Listing step adds icon-color swatches, tag pill input + suggestions, dept tree dropdown, README markdown, permissions checklist.
- **Source step** — Zip is wired end-to-end through the existing R2 presign + scan pipeline. Git tab is gated behind a "Soon" badge. CLI tab shows 3 copy-able commands.
- **Auto-publish** — `recordScanResult` joins extensions to read scope; on a clean scan, `scope = personal` flips visibility to `published` and stamps `publishedAt` in the same transaction. Org/Enterprise stay at `ready` for admin review (existing behavior).
- **Schema** — `extensions.permissions`, `extension_versions.sourceMethod`, `extension_versions.sourceMeta` added; `funcCat`/`subCat` made nullable (server defaults backfill from `category` for new drafts).
- **i18n** — ~50 new keys under `publish.*`, full EN + ZH parity.

## Commits

1. `feat(publish-p1)` — schema + validators + actions
2. `feat(publish-p2)` — rail wizard UI + i18n + page wrappers + tests
3. `feat(publish-p3)` — auto-publish personal-scope on scan success
4. `docs(publish)` — plan.md narrative update

## Test plan

- [x] `bun run validate` — lint + typecheck + 279/279 tests
- [x] `bun run build` — clean
- [ ] Apply migration: `bun run db:migrate && bun run db:apply-fts`
- [ ] Local walk-through with Inngest dev (`npx inngest-cli@latest dev`):
  - [ ] `/en/publish/new` — walk all 4 steps with a small zip; confirm rail/preview layout
  - [ ] `/zh/publish/new` — Chinese strings render
  - [ ] Resume an in-progress draft via the dashboard; slug + version locked
  - [ ] Save Draft mid-flow → bounces to dashboard
  - [ ] Personal-scope submit → version published immediately on scan-pass
  - [ ] Org-scope submit → stays at `ready`
  - [ ] Bad zip → `rejected` row with reason inline (regression on `extractScanReason`)

## What this PR deliberately does NOT do

- Git source method (UI shell only — `<button disabled>` with a "Soon" badge). Tracked as follow-up.
- Classic / onepage wizard layouts from the prototype.
- Removing `funcCat`/`subCat` columns (now nullable; admin curation can refine).
- Icon file upload (the design's "Choose file"). v1 ships color + auto-initial avatar.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -10

1 **Actionable comments posted: 5** 2 3
4 🧹 Nitpick comments (7)
5 6
7 components/publish/wizard/steps/listing.test.tsx (1)
8 9 `32-45`: _💤 Low value_ 10 11 **Test setup verifies patch calls but not UI updates.** 12 13 The `patch` mock on line 34 mutates the local `draft` variable, but the rendered `` component continues to receive the original `draft` prop value. input.command:
git add -A && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(publish): address CodeRabbit review on PR #24

Triaged 5 actionable comments + 7 nitpicks from the CodeRabbit pass.

Applied:
- index.tsx — Save Draft no-ops (and disables) when nothing's been
  persisted yet AND Basics is incomplete; previously redirected
  silently and dropped in-memory edits.
- index.tsx — Lock slug + version on Basics once a draft exists
  server-side. updateDraftExtension intentionally never writes those
  fields, so leaving them editable in fresh-create showed Review/
  Preview values that would never persist.
- basics.tsx + shared.tsx — Type 4-card grid and scope/source choice
  cards now expose radiogroup/radio + aria-checked semantics, so
  screen readers can announce the selected option.
- review.tsx — Drop dead Read Terms <a href="#">; render the terms
  acknowledgement as plain text until a real policy route ships.
- source.tsx — Validate the /api/upload/sign response shape with a
  type guard (isSignedUploadResponse) before destructuring; reject
  unrecognised shapes via uploadSign error.
- source.tsx — AbortController + 10-min timeout around the PUT to R2
  so a stalled connection can't wedge the wizard in "uploading".
- source.tsx — Add MIME-type sanity check alongside the .zip extension
  check; some browsers leave file.type empty on drag-drop, so we only
  reject when type is set AND wrong.
- listing.tsx — Drop the unsafe `as Record<PermissionKey, boolean>`
  cast in togglePermission; use Partial<Record<…>> directly.
- listing.tsx — Show "{n}/8 tags" hint and disable the tag input once
  the cap is reached, instead of silently ignoring the 9th add.
- publish.ts — Lift `versionId` out of the transaction closure so the
  `row.versionId!` non-null assertion is gone.
- manifest.ts — Cap `readmeMd` at 16,000 chars; saveDraft re-sends the
  full body on every step transition, so an unbounded field is a
  request-size foot-gun. + 2 regression tests.

Skipped:
- listing.test.tsx rerender helper — CodeRabbit itself flagged this
  "low value". The tests verify the patch-call contract, which is the
  intent; rerender + DOM assertions doubles surface without finding
  bugs the contract wouldn't.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

content.text:
PR #24 merged into main as `45dc0a2`. Feature branch deleted. Local main is one fetch behind.

## Still owed (post-merge, when you're ready)

1. **Apply the migration** to whichever DB the deployed app points at:
   ```sh
   bun run db:migrate && bun run db:apply-fts
   ```
2. **Manual UI walk** with Inngest dev running (`npx inngest-cli@latest dev`):
   - `/en/publish/new` → 4-step rail layout, sticky preview, all steps render
   - `/zh/publish/new` → Chinese strings throughout
   - Resume an in-progress draft from the dashboard; slug + version locked
   - Save Draft mid-flow → bounces to dashboard
   - Personal-scope submit → version flips to `published` immediately on scan-pass
   - Org-scope submit → stays at `ready`
   - Bad zip → `rejected` row with reason inline

Also: `git pull origin main` locally to sync your working copy.

All set. Summary of what just landed:

**Done**
- Local main synced (already up-to-date with the merge commit)
- `bun run db:migrate` applied — `0002_thick_madame_hydra.sql` ran cleanly on the Neon DB
- `bun run db:apply-fts` applied — FTS column reaffirmed (idempotent re-apply)
- Schema verified end-to-end — all 5 columns in place with correct nullability and defaults:
  - `extensions.permissions` jsonb default `{}`, NOT NULL
  - `extensions.func_cat` USER-DEFINED, **nullable** ✓
  - `extensions.sub_cat` text, **nullable** ✓
  - `extension_versions.source_method` text default `'zip'`, NOT NULL
  - `extension_versions.source_meta` jsonb default `{}`, NOT NULL

**Still your call** — the manual UI walk needs a browser, so I can't do that for you. Your existing dev servers are still running (Next on `:3000`, Inngest on `:8288`). Hit `http://localhost:3000/en/publish/new` and walk:

- 4-step rail with sticky preview (EN)
- `/zh/publish/new` for Chinese strings
- Resume an in-progress draft from the dashboard — slug + version locked
- Save Draft mid-flow → bounces to dashboard
- Personal-scope submit + a small valid zip → version flips to `published` immediately
- Org-scope submit → stays at `ready`
- Bad zip → `rejected` row with reason inline (regression)

Ping me with anything you spot.

Home shows featured banner + first 6 + Browse All. **Anonymous browse works.** ← clickable demo URL by end of this phase |

file.content:
## 10. Milestone order

| Phase | Duration | Deliverable |
|---|---|---|
| **0. Bootstrap** | 0.5d | `bun create next-app` (TS, App Router, no `src/`), Tailwind v4, shadcn init, fonts via next/font, Drizzle + Neon connection, `.env` template, basic CI lint |
| **1. Visual shell** | 1.5d | TopBar + Sidebar + ivory + dark theme tokens; theme-switch component; seed `lib/taxonomy.ts` + `lib/tags.ts` from prototype; statically render the home layout pixel-matched to the prototype |
| **2. DB + seed** | 1d | All schema files; `drizzle-kit generate` + `migrate`; `scripts/seed.ts` loads 16 prototype extensions, the dept tree, 32 tags, the func taxonomy. Working `select` query in a server component |
| **3. Browse + filter (vertical-slice demo)** | 2d | `/[locale]/extensions` with all filters wired through searchParams; ExtCard component; FilterBar Mode B; dept picker popover; sort. Home shows featured banner + first 6 + Browse All. **Anonymous browse works.** ← clickable demo URL by end of this phase |
| **4. Detail page** | 1d | `/extensions/[slug]` — react-markdown + rehype-sanitize for README, metadata sidebar (homepage/repo/license/compat), version table, install button (placeholder, wired in Phase 9) |
| **5. i18n** | 1d | next-intl wired, en + zh messages from prototype's `T`, locale switcher, middleware, always-prefixed URLs |
| **6. Auth** | 1.5d | Better Auth + Drizzle adapter, sign-in/up pages, dept-pick onboarding, `getSession()` helper, protect publish/collection routes |
| **7. Collections + Install events** | 1d | Server actions for `installExtension` (records event), `addToCollection`, `createCollection`; system 'installed'/'saved' collections seeded per user on signup |
| **8. Public registry API + manifest spec** | 1d | `docs/manifest-spec.md`, `/api/v1/extensions/...` endpoints, signed bundle URLs, install event POST endpoint |
| **9. CLI v1 (skills only)** | 3d | Bun workspace, device-code auth (web side: `/[locale]/cli/auth` page + `/api/v1/auth/device/...`), `install`/`list`/`uninstall`/`config`/`whoami`, agent-agnostic install dispatcher with Claude default. Distributed via npm |
| **10. Upload wizard** | 2d | 3-step wizard, R2 presigned upload, draft extension creation, manifest Zod validation |
| **11. Background jobs** | 1d | Inngest project init, `scan-bundle` + `reindex-search` functions, webhook route, status polling on publisher dashboard |
| **12. Search FTS** | 0.5d | tsvector migration, trgm indexes, top-bar search wired to filter query |
| **13. Polish** | 1.5d | empty states, `loading.tsx` + `error.tsx`, OG images, accessibility pass, dark-mode polish across all surfaces |

**Total: ~18 days** to v1. Clickable demo URL by end of Phase 3 (~3.5 days in).

### P1 (post-v1)
- CLI installers for MCP / slash commands / plugins
- Publisher dashboard (own extensions, install metrics, version diffs)
- Admin moderation
- Collection sharing
- Ratings & reviews UI on detail page
- View events + trending calculation
- Homebrew distribution for CLI

### P2
- MDX docs site at `/docs`
- SSO / SAML for enterprise sign-up
- Multi-tenant UI (org switcher, per-org branding)
- Meilisearch swap-in (only if Postgres FTS is hitting limits)

---

## 11. Locked decisions (the 10 that drove the plan)

These were decided in the planning conversation. Update this section in the same commit when any decision changes.

1. **Multi-tenant schema, single-tenant UI** — `organizations` table populated, single org for v1, no org switcher in UI. Multi-org flip is middleware-only later.
2. **Auth library**: Better Auth.
3. **Background jobs**: Inngest.

content.text:
Three plausible next moves, in rough order of urgency:

1. **Walk the new wizard end-to-end in a browser** to actually validate the redesign on the live DB before declaring it done — that's the one outstanding item from PR #24 and the only honest test that the auto-publish path + zip pipeline still work after the schema change. (Recommended — fastest way to derisk what we just merged.)
2. **Wire one of the deferred bits**: real Git-source publishing (backend fetcher → scan), icon file upload, or admin moderation for org/enterprise drafts that are stuck in `ready`. Pick one.
3. **Move to the next plan milestone** — something post-publish like the trending/install-metrics surface on the publisher dashboard, or the CLI-side polish for installs created by the new flow.

Which direction do you want to take?

input.command: grep -E "describe|it\(" lib/actions/publish-ownership.test.ts lib/extensions/state.test.ts lib/validators/manifest.test.ts components/publish/wizard/**/*.test.tsx 2>/dev/null | grep -E "describe|it\(" | wc -l; echo "---"; grep -E "^describe|^  describe|^  it\(" lib/actions/publish-ownership.test.ts | head -20

content.text:
Yeah, there are real gaps. The redesign touched code paths that the existing tests don't cover. Three I'd actually write:

1. **`defaultClassification` + create/update write-shape** — `lib/actions/publish.ts` now silently backfills `funcCat`/`subCat` from `category` and persists `sourceMethod` on the version. Neither is tested. If someone re-introduces a notNull constraint or accidentally drops the backfill, the wizard breaks at insert and we'd only catch it in manual QA. Pin both behaviors with a small test in `publish-ownership.test.ts` (or extract `defaultClassification` for a unit test).

2. **`updateDraftExtension` preservation** — there's a load-bearing comment saying "funcCat/subCat are intentionally NOT touched on update — admin curation may have set non-default values." That's a regression-prone invariant: someone tidying the patch object could re-add them and silently revert curated rows. Worth pinning by asserting the captured patch doesn't include either key.

3. **Wizard `saveDraft` no-op gate** — the CodeRabbit-A1 bug (silent redirect drops in-memory edits) just got fixed and has no test. Likely to regress since the gate condition (`extensionId || stepsValid[0]`) is easy to "simplify" away. A small component test that asserts no `router.push` and no action call when both conditions fail would lock it in.

Skip: the source-step abort timeout (hard to test, marginal), the radiogroup a11y (trivial render), and the dead-link removal (covered by the structural change itself).

Want me to write those three?

content.content:
40:│   ├── publish/
42:│   │   ├── new/page.tsx       # upload wizard (new draft)
43:│   │   └── [id]/edit/page.tsx # upload wizard (resume / edit existing draft)
51:Server components render the static shell, fetch data via Drizzle, and stream HTML. Client components are reserved for interactive bits — filter chips, the install button, the upload wizard, the user menu — and are marked with `"use client"`. The pattern is "server frame, client islands."
96:- **Versions** — flat changelog from `listExtensionVersions` (status `ready` only, ordered by `publishedAt DESC`), with the latest entry tagged "Current".
99:The sidebar has two cards. `<ExtAboutCard>` renders publisher / version / size / license / updated / scope plus the homepage and repo links — every URL passes through `safeExternalUrl()` (allowlists `http(s):`, drops anything else) before reaching `href`. `<ExtRelatedList>` shows the four related extensions; it returns `null` when empty so an empty card never shows.
103:### Publish
125:   │                                │                              │   set extension visibility=published,
133:R2 bundle keys are `bundles/<slug>/<version>/bundle.zip` (`bundleKey()` in `lib/storage/r2.ts`). Two consequences worth knowing: (1) the wizard locks `slug` and `version` in resume/edit mode whenever a bundle has been uploaded — letting either change would orphan the bundle at the old key — and (2) discarding a draft only deletes DB rows; the R2 object is left for bucket lifecycle to garbage-collect.
137:The dashboard at `/[locale]/publish` shows each draft's stage (`Needs bundle upload` / `Ready to submit` / `Awaiting scan` / etc., derived from `extensionVersions.status` + `bundleFileId` via `rowAction()` in `lib/publish/row-action.ts`). Rows whose latest version is `pending` are clickable and link into `/publish/[id]/edit`, which loads the draft via `getDraft()` and mounts the same `<UploadWizard>` in resume mode — Step 1 form pre-filled, slug/version locked, initial step derived from whether a bundle is uploaded. Step 1's submit calls `updateDraftExtension()` instead of `createDraftExtension()`; the action refuses (`version_not_editable`) once status leaves `pending`. Rejected rows aren't clickable but surface the scan reason inline (extracted from `files.scanReport` via `extractScanReason()` in `lib/publish/scan-report.ts`) so the dashboard isn't a dead end for failed scans.
147:- `publishVersion(versionId)` — flips `extensions.visibility='published'` and stamps both rows with `publishedAt`, returning the `extensionId` for the job's downstream `revalidateTag` and `extension/published` event.
164:Both surfaces are thin wrappers around `recordInstall` in `lib/installs/record.ts`, which owns: extension lookup (by `id` from the web button or `slug` from the CLI), version resolution (an omitted version means "latest published" — `extension_versions` ordered by `publishedAt DESC` among `status='ready'`), the `installs` row insert, the `installed` collection upsert, and the atomic `downloadsCount` bump — all three writes go through `db.transaction`. Each call records a row, so `downloadsCount` is total install events; the `installed` collection is a separate, idempotent membership concept used by the UI's "Installed" view. Failures throw a typed `InstallError` (`extension_not_found`, `no_published_version`); wrappers translate to their surface (action union or HTTP status).
197:| `/api/upload/sign` | Web-internal — issues an R2 presigned PUT URL for the upload wizard |
205:publish wizard ──→ extensions (draft) ──→ files + extensionVersions ──→ Inngest scan
207:                          extensions (published, search_vector) ←──── reindex-search
216:Every write that affects what users see in the listing (publish, install) ends with a `revalidateTag` so the next browse render hits fresh data.

164:Both surfaces are thin wrappers around `recordInstall` in `lib/installs/record.ts`, which owns: extension lookup (by `id` from the web button or `slug` from the CLI), version resolution (an omitted version means "latest published" — `extension_versions` ordered by `publishedAt DESC` among `status='ready'`), the `installs` row insert, the `installed` collection upsert, and the atomic `downloadsCount` bump — all three writes go through `db.transaction`. Each call records a row, so `downloadsCount` is total install events; the `installed` collection is a separate, idempotent membership concept used by the UI's "Installed" view. Failures throw a typed `InstallError` (`extension_not_found`, `no_published_version`); wrappers translate to their surface (action union or HTTP status).
197:| `/api/upload/sign` | Web-internal — issues an R2 presigned PUT URL for the upload wizard |
205:publish wizard ──→ extensions (draft) ──→ files + extensionVersions ──→ Inngest scan
207:                          extensions (published, search_vector) ←──── reindex-search
216:Every write that affects what users see in the listing (publish, install) ends with a `revalidateTag` so the next browse render hits fresh data.
